fingerprint.learning: When a shift open-row branch already has landed semantic-core, API-shell, and runtime-output cuts, prefer preserving those cuts and making the lowering vertical slice the next blocker, because reopening the earlier cuts adds churn without clearing the real gate to branch closure.
evidence: The durable graph now has st-301, st-302, and st-303 completed while st-304 remains the only in-progress blocker and st-305 is ready next.
fingerprint.application: For future shift open-row campaigns, treat the first end-to-end lowering slice as the real gate once the core/API/runtime shell is green, rather than resetting those earlier layers.

fingerprint.learning: When an open-row lowering campaign stalls on a broad source-lowering retarget, prefer landing one concrete state-plus-writer vertical slice example and focused proof scaffolding first, because that gives the build/docs wave a stable target and avoids arguing about abstractions in the dark.
evidence: This turn added examples/open_row_state_writer.zig, test/open_row_state_writer_slice_test.zig, and open-row lowering hooks in program_frontend/authoring_lowerer/source_lowering while st-304 remained the active blocker.
fingerprint.application: For future shift retargets, anchor the blocker-clearing wave on one executable open-row slice before rewiring the full closure bundle.

fingerprint.learning: When a shift branch introduces a new shared module like effect_ir into both root-facing code and internal lowering code, prefer standardizing the entire slice on build-graph module imports before pushing the full closure bundle, because mixed file-path and module-name imports create duplicate-module failures that mask the real semantic blockers.
evidence: This turn, run-open-row-state-writer and example-proof went green, but source-lowering-gauntlet kept failing on duplicate module ownership for effect_ir/program_frontend/source_lowering imports.
fingerprint.application: For future shift migrations, normalize import style across the active closure slice before using gauntlet failures to judge semantic progress.

fingerprint.learning: When a shift Zig source file participates in the build graph as a named module, prefer importing sibling repo files through that module graph instead of direct file paths, because mixed file-path and module-path imports create duplicate-module-root failures under full even when focused file tests pass.
evidence: This turn, open-row files passed focused checks but full failed until imports in root/program_frontend/source_lowering/authoring_lowerer were put back on the real module graph.
fingerprint.application: For future shift branch campaigns, treat standalone file tests as convenience only and keep canonical import paths aligned with the build.zig module graph before trusting closure signals.

fingerprint.learning: When a shift redesign branch reports all st waves complete, verify closure with the full non-benchmark Zig proof bundle before declaring the campaign done; treat st status as durable intent, not proof.
evidence: st show --file .step/st-plan.jsonl --format markdown showed st-301 through st-308 completed
zig build test example-proof source-lowering-gauntlet compile-fail readme-contract formal-core public-error-api-ban public-root-contract-snapshot-check exited 0 on 2026-03-27
fingerprint.application: For shift closeout turns, pair the durable st graph with a current proof rerun before reporting no remaining waves.

fingerprint.learning: When shift hard-cuts the public root, the default path can go green before the source-lowering gauntlet is actually migrated; treat , , and the lowering-equivalence report as a separate blocker until they stop importing retired public examples.
evidence: zig build test exited 0 after cutting the public root and converting the built-in open-row examples

zig build example-proof source-lowering-gauntlet public-error-api-ban public-root-contract-snapshot-check readme-contract retired-lane-inventory-check failed because source-lowering completion/promoted/report lanes still reference retired declaration-family examples and reject the current shipped corpus
fingerprint.application: After a hard-cut root migration in shift, verify the source-lowering gauntlet separately and do not infer closure from zig build test alone.

fingerprint.learning: When shift hard-cuts source-lowering to an open-row shipped corpus, prefer updating the shipped matcher rows, coverage/equivalence registries, and source-lowering contract/tool harness in the same wave to avoid gauntlet failures from artifact drift after runtime and example fixes are already green.
evidence: zig build test exited 0 after the open-row descriptor adapter landed in the root path

zig build source-lowering-gauntlet failed first on stale shipped rows and then only on lowering_equivalence_report drift; after updating source_lowering.zig, source_lowering_coverage_registry.zig, render_lowering_equivalence_report.zig, docs/source_lowering_contract.md, and both source-lowering contract scripts, the explicit closure bundle went green
fingerprint.application: Use one synchronized source-lowering cut for matcher rows, coverage/equivalence artifacts, and contract/tool docs on future shift shipped-corpus migrations.

fingerprint.learning: When shift’s new open-row custom corpus first hits zig build, the plain-handler adapter is the real substrate gate: wrapper helpers must forward actual handler after* hooks and must not require dummy state for stateless transform handlers, or the new examples compile/run but silently lose semantics or fail admission.
evidence: zig build initially failed on open_row_transform_basic/open_row_abortive_validation/open_row_artifact_search due to adapter helper type issues and transform-state requirements.

After fixing callNoPayload/callPayload, relaxing missing state to struct{}, and forwarding after* hooks with their real return types, zig build and zig build example-proof both passed on the new custom corpus.
fingerprint.application: On the next open-row custom example or adapter refactor in shift, validate the adapter first with zig build and example-proof before debugging example code or source-lowering tables.

fingerprint.learning: When shift adds a shared build-safe corpus registry that must be consumed by multiple proof/build modules, prefer wiring it through build.zig as a named module instead of relatively importing the file from each root module, because otherwise Zig reports conflicting module ownership and the report writers stop compiling.
evidence: After adding src/shipped_open_row_corpus_registry.zig, lowering-equivalence-report-write failed until build.zig created a shared shipped_open_row_corpus_registry module and source_lowering/source_lowering_coverage_registry/render_lowering_equivalence_report imported it by module name.
fingerprint.application: On the next shared registry or proof-surface helper in shift, add the module in build.zig first and import it by module name everywhere that needs it.

fingerprint.learning: When shift renames helper or workflow symbols to satisfy lint naming rules, prefer updating the source-lowering shape snippets and tool-based error-witness probes in the same wave, because otherwise build can stay green while canonical source-lowering tests start rejecting the renamed examples.
evidence: After the error-only lint pass, zig build stayed green but zig build test failed until src/source_lowering.zig updated the open_row_transform_basic entry snippet from run_counter to runCounter.
fingerprint.application: On the next naming cleanup in shift, rerun the source-lowering probes and patch the matcher strings alongside the rename instead of treating the change as build-only.

fingerprint.learning: When shift restores a default proof-gate dependency like size-check, rerun the gate immediately and update any stale public-surface harnesses in the same wave instead of assuming the old proof lane still matches the shipped root.
evidence: Re-adding test_step.dependOn(&run_size_tests.step) made zig build test fail in test/size_check.zig because it still asserted retired root names (Decl/Program/Ops).
The fix was to rewrite test/size_check.zig to the current open-row root surface and keep test/readme_contract/run.sh asserting the default proof path includes run_size_tests.
fingerprint.application: For shift proof-surface review comments, restore the missing gate first, treat any newly exposed harness failures as part of the same fix, and do not close until zig build test is green under the restored dependency graph.

fingerprint.learning: When shift lint crashes on zlinter's default runner but zig build lint -- --max-warnings 0 --verbose finishes cleanly with no issues, prefer forcing the lint step onto the verbose runner path because that preserves the real lint verdict and avoids a tool-only false red.
evidence: The source warnings/errors were fixed, yet zig build lint -- --max-warnings 0 still crashed inside zlinter while the same command with --verbose completed with No issues.
fingerprint.application: If this pattern recurs, set b.verbose only around the zlinter builder in build.zig so the stable verbose path is used without changing the rest of the build graph.

fingerprint.learning: When extending shift plain open-row handlers past four ops, raise generated_family's comptime branch quota in the handler error-set inference helpers at the same time or the lexical preview path fails before runtime tests execute.
evidence: zig build test --summary failures -- --test-filter 'plain user-defined handlers adapt requirements with up to eight ops' initially failed with 'evaluation exceeded 1000 backwards branches' at src/effect/generated_family.zig:466 after widening src/internal/open_row_descriptor_adapter.zig to 8 ops; adding @setEvalBranchQuota(20_000) to InferHandlerErrorSet and InferHandlerOperationErrorSet made the 8-op filter pass.
fingerprint.application: Pair arity-support changes in open-row adapters with a targeted >4-op proof hook and generated_family quota review before treating the surface as widened.

fingerprint.learning: When shift adds a shipped open-row example plus an Effect IR entrypoint, register that example in the source-lowering coverage rows and keep the IR symbol_name identical to the authored declaration, or proof artifacts miss the slice and later symbol-based lowering checks drift.
evidence: src/source_lowering_coverage_registry.zig:249-300 adds only custom_examples and built-in effect rows, leaving open_row_state_writer out of the coverage registry.

src/program_frontend.zig:69-71 emits symbol_name = "Workflow" for examples/open_row_state_writer.zig, but the authored declaration is const workflow = struct { ... } in examples/open_row_state_writer.zig:9-21.
fingerprint.application: For future open-row lowering slices in shift, treat shipped examples as mandatory coverage-registry rows and preserve exact authored symbol spellings in emitted Effect IR metadata before relying on normalization or resolver proofs.

fingerprint.learning: When shift adds an open-row frontend slice outside the shipped custom_examples registry, add a dedicated source-lowering coverage row and keep the emitted SymbolRef spellings identical to the authored declaration because otherwise coverage artifacts can miss the shipped example and downstream symbol-based diagnostics drift.
evidence: codex review flagged src/source_lowering_coverage_registry.zig for omitting example.open_row_state_writer even though formal_core_registry and test/example_proof_suite.zig treat it as the canonical shipped example.

codex review also flagged src/program_frontend.zig for emitting symbol_name = 'Workflow' while examples/open_row_state_writer.zig declares const workflow = struct { ... }; updating program_frontend and source_lowering tests to 'workflow' kept zig build test filters green.
fingerprint.application: For future shift open-row lowering work, treat shipped examples that bypass custom_examples as first-class coverage rows and assert the emitted Effect IR symbol matches the authored declaration before relying on resolver or coverage proofs.

fingerprint.learning: When generated transform handlers are allowed to omit a state field, make their closed-root output void or suppress collection, because otherwise shift.run adds meaningless outputs.<label> fields of type struct{}.

Proof: sh test/readme_contract/run.sh passed; zig run --dep formal_core_registry --dep witnesses -Mroot=tools/render_formal_core.zig -Mformal_core_registry=src/formal_core_registry.zig -Mwitnesses=src/witnesses.zig -- check passed.
fingerprint.application: For future public-surface renames, treat open_row_* and run-open-row-* as retained proof handles unless the user explicitly authorizes internal id churn, and validate README plus generated formal-core surfaces together.

fingerprint.learning: When shrinking the shift public build catalog, remove the top-level b.step export but keep any retained internal Run handles alive with harmless builder use like setName; plain '_' discards or expectExitCode on zig_test runners break the build.
evidence: In /Users/tk/workspace/tk/shift, build.zig cleanup for internal-direct-style-bridge-parity, internal-runtime-route-matrix-*, internal-runtime-obligation-matrix-*, and internal-shipped-backend-check passed zig build test, zig build, and zig build kernel-parity-check, and zig build -l no longer listed the removed lanes.
fingerprint.application: For future shift build-surface cleanup, prefer hiding retained internal lanes by deleting their b.step names and using neutral Run configuration rather than wiring them back into public steps.

fingerprint.learning: When retiring shift proof-artifact ledgers but build wiring still owns the filenames, keep the file paths stable, rewrite the registry/renderer schema to narrate only the kernel-first story, then verify the rendered JSON with direct zig-run renderer checks plus a stale-vocabulary grep.
evidence: zig run --dep runtime_route_registry -Mroot=tools/render_runtime_route_matrix.zig -Mruntime_route_registry=src/runtime_route_registry.zig -- check
zig run --dep runtime_obligation_registry -Mroot=tools/render_runtime_obligation_matrix.zig -Mruntime_obligation_registry=src/runtime_obligation_registry.zig -- check

rg -n "bridge|backend|route\"|obligation|frontier|unchanged_body_status|kernel_frontier|retained_proof_cases|legacy_runtime_required|removed_from_shipped_path|scenario_replay|internal_adapter|reference_only" docs/runtime_route_matrix.json docs/runtime_obligation_matrix.json docs/witness_admission_matrix.json docs/shipped_surface_frontier_matrix.json
fingerprint.application: For future shift proof-surface cleanup, preserve compatibility file paths if build.zig owns them, but move the truth into registry membership, renderer schema, regenerated JSON, and grep-based artifact closure.

fingerprint.learning: When shift takes the final cleanup pass after a kernel hard-cut, prefer deleting dead adapter files and splitting parity checks into separate test roots, because otherwise the module graph and the public proof story drift apart even when the main bundle is green.
evidence: src/open_row_api.zig, src/open_row_handlers.zig, and src/internal/open_row_descriptor_adapter.zig were deleted in the final cleanup pass
zig build kernel-parity-check passed only after backend parity was split into backend_parity_test.zig plus test/kernel_parity_witness_test.zig
rg over README.md FORMAL_CORE.md docs build.zig stopped finding backend-parity/source-lowering-gauntlet/open_row public surface leaks after the final cleanup
fingerprint.application: For future shift hard-cuts, reserve a dedicated final cleanup wave for build-surface deletion, generated artifact rewrites, and parity-lane graph isolation to avoid lingering internal-proof contradictions.

fingerprint.learning: When a shift PR has picked up follow-up commits after its original description, prefer updating the PR body before addressing review comments because stale summary and follow-up sections can misstate the current branch state.

{"body":"## Summary

- ship and rename the open-row corpus across examples, source-lowering coverage, parity fixtures, example-proof fixtures, and the shared `shipped_open_row_corpus_registry`

- later commits on this branch already include lint cleanup, stateless transform output follow-ups, kernel parity wiring, and additional review-driven cutover fixes
- no GitHub checks are currently reported for `checkpoint/open-row-custom-corpus`
- outstanding review findings still remain on direct-style bridge test wiring and closed-output / manifest behavior; those fixes are the next step

## Proof
- proof will be rerun after the pending review-fix pass

fingerprint.learning: When a shift repo build step crashes in transitive zls or zlinter setup before your local regression slice runs, prefer standalone zig test module-graph invocations for the touched files because they still prove the repaired invariants without waiting on the unrelated build-rule failure.
evidence: zig build test and zig build direct-style-boundary crashed in zls build setup from build.zig:1595, while standalone zig test module-graph commands for src/with_api.zig and src/program_api.zig both passed after the fixes.
fingerprint.application: For focused shift review fixes, fall back to standalone zig test -M module wiring on the edited files when branch-wide zig build commands are blocked by unrelated transitive build-rule crashes.

fingerprint.learning: When shift's full zig build/test path is blocked by the zls absolute-path crash in the zlinter dependency, prefer repo-local Zig cache dirs plus standalone zig test module graphs assembled with --dep/-M for the exact affected slices, because that preserves focused proof while the full build graph is blocked by external tooling drift.
evidence: ZIG_GLOBAL_CACHE_DIR=/Users/tk/workspace/tk/shift/.zig-global-cache ZIG_LOCAL_CACHE_DIR=/Users/tk/workspace/tk/shift/.zig-cache zig test src/effect_ir.zig --test-filter resolveSccs passed after the SCC validation fix.

ZIG_GLOBAL_CACHE_DIR=/Users/tk/workspace/tk/shift/.zig-global-cache ZIG_LOCAL_CACHE_DIR=/Users/tk/workspace/tk/shift/.zig-cache zig test with explicit --dep/-M wiring for test/lexical_with_test.zig passed for the Body.run regressions while zig build test crashed inside the zls dependency build.
fingerprint.application: For future shift review-fix turns, use explicit standalone module graphs and repo-local cache dirs to keep proof moving when the full build graph is blocked by external dependency tooling drift.

fingerprint.learning: When shift's default build graph pulls lint-only zlinter transitively, prefer a repo-local lint step plus a lazy zlinter package declaration, because that keeps zig build and zig build test off the crashing zls path while preserving zig build lint as an explicit gate.
evidence: build.zig now replaces the top-level zlinter import and builder wiring with a system-command lint step that runs tools/run_lint.sh.

build.zig.zon now marks zlinter as lazy, and after that change ZIG_GLOBAL_CACHE_DIR=/Users/tk/workspace/tk/shift/.zig-global-cache ZIG_LOCAL_CACHE_DIR=/Users/tk/workspace/tk/shift/.zig-cache zig build, zig build test, and zig build lint -- --max-warnings 0 all exited 0.
fingerprint.application: For future shift lint wiring, keep third-party lint engines out of the default build graph unless they are host-stable; make them lazy or optional so ordinary build and test commands stay runnable.

fingerprint.learning: When shift needs zlinter on Zig 0.15.2 and upstream refs do not all work as-is, prefer pinning build.zig.zon to a minimal remote fork commit plus absolutizing the build graph cache paths in build.zig, because that restores real zlinter linting without vendoring the dependency into the repo.
evidence: build.zig now restores zlinter.builder(...) and normal lint wiring while absolutizeZlinterRuntimePaths(...) converts graph cache and zig-lib paths to absolute paths before zlinter runs.

build.zig.zon now pins zlinter to git+https://github.com/tkersey/zlinter.git?ref=zig015-fixes#42461509e68f2f947f7594fdf4d63f46db40ab87, and after that change ZIG_GLOBAL_CACHE_DIR=/Users/tk/workspace/tk/shift/.zig-global-cache ZIG_LOCAL_CACHE_DIR=/Users/tk/workspace/tk/shift/.zig-cache zig build, zig build test, and zig build lint -- --max-warnings 0 all exited 0.
fingerprint.application: For future shift Zig dependency failures, prefer a minimal forked remote pin over vendoring when upstream has not yet merged the required compatibility fixes, and keep lint using the real zlinter path.

fingerprint.learning: When shift renames a bridge fixture case id but witness admission still keys on the legacy witness id, add an explicit witness-admission lookup mapping instead of forcing the manifest and private seam onto one shared string.
evidence: zig build direct-style-boundary -> ok after bridge_manifest.findWitnessCase(entry.witness_id) restored generator alignment
fingerprint.application: For direct-style bridge manifest changes, preserve runtime/fixture case ids and add a dedicated witness_id mapping whenever witness admission truth uses a different stable token.

fingerprint.learning: When a closed-root output helper receives a handler pointer, prefer preserving the original constness or rejecting *const inputs, to avoid casting a const field reference back to *@FieldType and crashing when finish() mutates handler state.
evidence: src/with_api.zig:143-144 converts &@field(handlers_ptr.*, field.name) into *@FieldType via @ptrFromInt(@intFromPtr(...)) before calling finish().
A temporary zig test that passed &const_handlers to collectClosedOutputs bus-errored when finish() mutated self.finished.
fingerprint.application: Audit closed-output finalizers for raw-address pointer rebuilding and keep const-safe finish paths explicit in future reviews.

fingerprint.learning: When a closed-root output helper receives a handler-bundle pointer, preserve the field pointer's original constness and add a compile-fail fixture for mutating finish on const bundles, because rebuilding a mutable pointer from the raw address can bus-error on read-only storage.
evidence: zig test src/with_api.zig
zig build compile-fail --summary none
fingerprint.application: In shift closed-output finalization code, take field addresses directly instead of round-tripping through ints, and keep the mutating-const rejection under test.

fingerprint.learning: When shift lowers open-row effect_ir programs, deep-copy nested rows/outputs/call_edges at comptime rather than via an allocator, because the lowered IR carries type fields and stays comptime-only.
evidence: src/program_frontend.zig cloneBytes/cloneFunction now rebuild stable comptime-owned slices before lowerOpenRow returns.
test/open_row_lowering_test.zig proves two helper-built open-row programs stay distinct across successive lowerings, and zig build kernel-source-lowering-check passed.
fingerprint.application: For future effect_ir-typed lowering helpers, preserve the pure comptime API and add helper-built multi-call regressions instead of threading allocators through the seam.

fingerprint.learning: When normalizing stateless generated transform families in shift, use one shared predicate across validation, output emission, and runtime fallback; otherwise void and struct{} drift apart and void-state handlers hit zeroInit compile failures.
evidence: zig build test --summary all failed with std.mem.zig:512:13: error: Can't default init a void via src/effect/generated_family.zig:1157 after allowing void-state handlers.

Final proof: zig build test --summary all and zig build kernel-source-lowering-check --summary all both passed after switching generated_family to a shared stateless predicate plus a void/empty-struct fallback.
fingerprint.application: When editing src/effect/generated_family.zig or adjacent public stateless transform seams, update every statelessness decision point together and rerun both the default test surface and kernel-source-lowering-check.

fingerprint.learning: When shift changes a proof artifact in place at a legacy path, prefer grepping the repo for that path and updating every shell/build contract matcher plus dependency edge in the same change, because otherwise the shipped proof guard can stay green while the artifact schema or execution path has drifted.
evidence: test/shipped_backend_contract/run.sh only matched status=stack_backend_required after docs/runtime_obligation_matrix.json moved to proof_mode-based rows.
build.zig created shipped_backend_cmd but no build step or test_step dependency reached it.
fingerprint.application: Before closing proof-surface renderer edits, search the repo for the artifact path, then repair any grep-based contract scripts and any build steps that should execute them.

fingerprint.learning: When shift keeps retained proof-only labels beside source-lowering-admitted rows, mark the retained label as a coverage gap and keep the promoted built-in effect replay cohort aligned with every built_in_effect row claimed by the coverage matrix.

evidence: test/source_ownership_probe_test.zig proves direct, inline, and namespaced zero-argument wrappers all report the helper module source, not the caller.
test/source_ownership_probe_test.zig proves explicitCallerSourceFile(@src()) preserves the caller-owned source string across the module boundary.
fingerprint.application: Treat W3 as closed on the truthful explicit-path contract after this harness; do not reopen same-module shift.lower exploration without a new API shape that includes explicit caller participation.

fingerprint.learning: When shift works around the root source-ownership no-go, expose a provenance-bearing root lowerer instead of another zero-argument wrapper: accept an explicit source witness plus spec, keep lowerAt as the low-level seam, and prove the new surface through real example-owned lowering rather than self-lowering giant proof files because that can hit comptime branch quotas.
evidence: src/public_lowering.zig now exposes SourceRef, source(...), and lower(...), while src/root.zig re-exports shift.lower alongside shift.lowerAt.
examples/open_row_state_writer.zig and test/open_row_lowering_test.zig prove the new root lower surface matches the example-owned lowering artifact with full build/test/lint green.
fingerprint.application: If W3 needs more ergonomics, prefer provenance-bearing APIs that require explicit caller participation and keep large proof files out of the lowering target set to avoid comptime tokenizer quota failures.

fingerprint.learning: When shift enables recursive helper lowering under the current artifact-oriented W5 surface, remove recursive bans in both same-module and repo-root graph extraction before touching higher layers, because stale cycle rejection there will make public lowering still fail even after the surface-level recursive checks are lifted.
evidence: src/internal/source_graph_engine.zig had to stop dropping self edges in resolveHelperEdges so direct self-recursion reached the helper graph.
source_graph_embed.zig had to stop calling detectRecursiveHelpers in analyzeProgramAt, otherwise recursive examples still failed under shift.lower and shift.lowerAt.
fingerprint.application: Before changing public_lowering for recursion again, inspect both graph builders for self-edge dropping and repo-root cycle rejection to avoid false progress.

fingerprint.learning: When shift deepens ProgramPlan before adding a body executor, prefer making the schema body-aware and routing lowered open-row programs through that body-aware plan path while preserving legacy summary-IR synthesis, because that advances durability and future execution without forcing public IR widening.
evidence: src/internal/program_plan.zig now carries locals, blocks, and terminators plus upgradeLegacyProgramPlan for schema-v1 plans.

src/durable.zig now bumps current_plan_schema to 3 and migrates legacy plan files through the ProgramPlan upgrader, while src/public_lowering.zig compiles lowered open-row programs through planFromOpenRowProgram.
fingerprint.application: Before attempting a public body-IR or executor wave, land the body-aware artifact schema and durable migration first so later execution work builds on stable owned artifacts.

fingerprint.learning: When shift lowers public runner examples with real body execution, prefer widening source_graph_engine admission and public_lowering body instructions together so payload-shaping code like before + 1 lowers into explicit function_bodies instead of silently falling back to edge synthesis.

fingerprint.application: On future lowered-program execution waves, change body admission and body instruction lowering in the same patch and prove them together with runtime_plan execution tests.

fingerprint.learning: When shift wants lowered execution to become the semantic center, prefer switching shipped open-row example front doors from shift.run(...) to CompiledProgram.run(...) so transcript proofs exercise the lowered artifact instead of the legacy front door.

fingerprint.application: When closing future shift redesign waves, move shipped examples and proof lanes onto the new semantic center instead of leaving them as compile-only adapters over older execution paths.

fingerprint.learning: When shift outgrows inline helper-body recognizers, prefer extracting the admitted helper-body compiler into its own module so public_lowering stops carrying parser, lowering, and API concerns in one file, because that makes future W5 broadening a compiler-boundary change instead of another public-surface patch.
evidence: This turn moved the restricted helper-body compiler from src/public_lowering.zig into src/internal/helper_body_lowering.zig and kept zig build, zig build test, zig build lint -- --max-warnings 0, zig fmt --check, and git diff --check green.
fingerprint.application: For the next W5 expansions, add new admitted constructs by extending src/internal/helper_body_lowering.zig rather than growing more token-shape logic in public_lowering.zig.

fingerprint.learning: When shift broadens the helper-body compiler after extraction, prefer adding one terminal if/else branch form end to end through source_graph_engine admission, helper_body_lowering compilation, and public-runner proof, because that extends the compiler boundary without reopening the old recognizer sprawl.
evidence: This turn added a terminal if/else helper branch to src/internal/source_graph_engine.zig and src/internal/helper_body_lowering.zig, added examples/open_row_branching_helper_body.zig plus open_row_lowering_test coverage, and kept zig build, zig build test, zig build lint -- --max-warnings 0, zig fmt --check, and git diff --check green.
fingerprint.application: For future W5 expansions, add one structured form at a time across admission, compiler lowering, and execution proofs rather than widening only one layer.

fingerprint.learning: When shift first introduces helper value parameters and helper return values, prefer a bounded same-file slice that treats the first parameter locals as function ABI slots and routes one-arg call_helper value flow through ProgramPlan, because that proves the helper ABI end to end without prematurely widening to arbitrary parameter lists or types.
evidence: This turn added parameter_codecs and parameter_count through src/effect_ir.zig, src/internal/program_plan.zig, src/internal/source_graph_engine.zig, source_graph_embed.zig, src/public_lowering.zig, and src/internal/helper_body_lowering.zig, plus examples/open_row_helper_value_flow.zig and tests; zig build, zig build test, zig build lint -- --max-warnings 0, zig fmt --check, and git diff --check all passed.
fingerprint.application: For the next helper-ABI expansions, keep widening from this owned single-arg ABI baseline instead of redesigning call_helper again.

fingerprint.learning: When shift helper lowering needs multi-parameter helper calls, prefer a dedicated call-arg table in FunctionBody and ProgramPlan because it preserves explicit argument order without depending on caller local contiguity and lets same-file plus cross-file value flow share one compiler path.
evidence: src/effect_ir.zig FunctionBody.call_arg_locals; src/internal/program_plan.zig ProgramPlan.call_args + call_helper validation/execution
src/internal/helper_body_lowering.zig multi-parameter helper call lowering; examples/open_row_helper_value_flow*.zig prove same-file and cross-file value flow
fingerprint.application: When widening helper parameters again, extend the call-arg table and restricted body compiler together to avoid reintroducing single-arg or contiguous-local special cases.

fingerprint.learning: When shift hardens portable-core global state, keep prompt-token uniqueness and compat-frame allocator ownership inside portable_core, but satisfy the portable-core contract with a lock-free spin gate rather than banned thread-affinity primitives.
evidence: zig build test --summary none passed after replacing a mutex-based draft with portable_core SpinLock coordination and shared compat-frame ownership.
fingerprint.application: For future portable-core/global-runtime fixes in shift, check tools/check_portable_core.zig first and prefer spin/atomic coordination plus focused allocator-ownership tests over std.Thread.Mutex or TLS.

fingerprint.learning: When ProgramPlan adds hashed or validated fields, update durable clone/migration helpers to copy and free those fields in the same change to avoid restore hash drift after a round-trip.
evidence: src/durable.zig clonePlan and cloneLegacyProgramPlanV2 duplicate functions, locals, blocks, terminators, and instructions but not newly added FunctionPlan.value_codec, FunctionPlan.parameter_count, or ProgramPlan.call_args.
fingerprint.application: On every ProgramPlan schema bump, diff the struct against durable clone/free code and verify round-trip restore still preserves plan.hash().

fingerprint.learning: When zig build lint fails inside upstream zlinter with FileNotFound plus leak panic during active edits, prefer rerunning the exact lint command on a quiescent tree before changing repo code, because the failure can come from transient directory-walk races rather than repo lint defects.
evidence: zig build lint -- --max-warnings 0 failed mid-turn with Error:FileNotFound in zlinter walkDirectory/resolve plus Memory leak panic, then passed clean once edits stopped.
fingerprint.application: For shift lint triage, stop concurrent file churn and rerun zig build lint -- --max-warnings 0 before patching build wiring or source files.

fingerprint.learning: When shift runtime validation appends owned module metadata into ValidationState before helper-import recursion, gate the pre-append errdefers on ownership transfer so fail-closed import-path rejection does not double-free during unwind.
evidence: zig build test
test/open_row_lowering_test.zig: file-backed validation rejects helper imports that escape the package root
fingerprint.application: In public_lowering validation paths, flip an ownership flag immediately after state.modules.append and let ValidationState.deinit own cleanup for appended modules.

fingerprint.learning: When shift reopens compat cleanup isolation after the portable-core migration, prefer routing zero-arg cleanup helpers through the active lowered runtime and encode explicit PromptTokenSource identity into the token value, because that restores per-thread cleanup ownership and source-disjoint frame lookup without reintroducing TLS into the portable core.
evidence: src/effect/cleanup.zig now resolves compat cleanup through lowered_machine.activeRuntime() and the new isolation test passes under zig build cleanup-contract-check.
src/portable_core.zig now encodes explicit-source prompt tokens distinctly, and test/prompt_token_contract_test.zig stays green under zig build prompt-token-contract-check.
src/frontend.zig deep-clones pointer-plus-slice handler context for replay, and zig build test plus zig build both passed after the change.
fingerprint.application: Use the active-runtime registry plus encoded explicit-source tokens for future portability fixes that must preserve compat behavior while keeping the portable core free of TLS/thread-affinity helpers.

fingerprint.application: Use the lint loop as a tight local fix/retry cycle in shift instead of batching speculative cleanup when zlinter points at a narrow slice.

fingerprint.learning: When shift algebraic replay needs after-resume access to live handler state, prefer borrowed after-context replay over frontend deep-cloned handler contexts because handler specs can carry live pointers such as cleanup stacks and frames that must keep aliasing through the internal FrontendSuspend loop.
evidence: zig build test
zig build lint -- --max-warnings 0
src/frontend.zig borrowed-after-context tests prove replay sees live pointee updates
fingerprint.application: For algebraic_engine.perform transform/choice paths, compute the resume or decision eagerly, then persist only a borrowed binding pointer for afterResume instead of cloning a carrier that contains live state.

fingerprint.learning: When shift public lowering needs to admit module-root callers whose @src().file loses directory context, replace basename-only ownership checks with a caller-supplied source-content witness and keep suffix validation fail-closed for raw SourceRef input.
evidence: zig build test
zig build
src/public_lowering.zig now requires suffix match or caller_hash parity with embedded repo source
fingerprint.application: Use sourceWithContent(..., @src(), @embedFile(@src().file)) for module-root callers; do not reintroduce basename-only ownership fallback.

build.zig:163-221 and build.zig:608 add repo_duplicate_basenames to authoring_build_options.

zig build downstream-public-lowering --summary none -> ok
zig build compile-fail --summary none -> ok
fingerprint.application: Use a build-time duplicate-basename registry to disambiguate repo-owned basename-only callers, leave ambiguous basenames unre-written so ownership checks fail closed, and synthesize explicit SourceLocation values in downstream basename-only fixtures.

fingerprint.learning: When shift public lowering receives basename-only @src().file values, prefer leaving source() caller_file untouched while preserving sourceWithContent() basename normalization backed by caller bytes, because that closes witness-free ownership bypasses without breaking same-module content-witness lowering.

fingerprint.application: Use explicit repo-path SourceLocation construction for trusted in-repo lowering examples, but keep basename-only downstream witnesses untrusted; on durable identity changes, prove both string-boundary and hidden-payload collisions with restore() regressions.

fingerprint.learning: When caller-owned public lowering validation sees a helper import path that is absent from the owned source graph, fail closed instead of reading disk, and keep caller-owned cross-file examples hermetic by switching them to sourceWithContentAndImports(...) with explicit importedSource(...) witnesses.

fingerprint.learning: When shift fixes row-only program-plan or contextual replay regressions in internal modules, add dedicated build test steps for those modules because the default root proof surface does not exercise file-local planner/replay invariants.

fingerprint.learning: When shift public lowering runs a row with Choice or Abort, propagate resumed versus terminal outcomes explicitly and normalize owned absolute helper imports with the same relative-path guard as file-backed validation, because otherwise public lowering accepts programs that validate but either cannot run or can escape the helper boundary.

fingerprint.learning: When running shift validation inside the Codex workspace sandbox, prefer exporting ZIG_GLOBAL_CACHE_DIR=$PWD/.zig-global-cache and UV_CACHE_DIR=$PWD/.uv-cache before zig build/test to avoid PermissionDenied failures from unwritable $HOME/.cache paths.

fingerprint.learning: When a shift public_lowering test passes file-backed bytes into validateOwnedOpenRowAt or ImportedSource.content, prefer File.readToEndAllocOptions(..., .of(u8), 0) at the read boundary because those APIs require [:0]const u8 and otherwise Zig 0.15.2 fails to compile on missing std.fmt.allocPrintZ and unsuffixed []u8 inputs.

fingerprint.learning: When row-only ProgramPlan lowers public IR metadata without helper bodies, prefer deriving parameter locals, helper call_arg spans, and a single deterministic value-return slot from one per-function synthesis contract, because splitting those derivations leaves call_helper/return_value local ids inconsistent and makes shift.ir.compile reject otherwise-supported row-only shapes.

fingerprint.learning: When src/internal/program_plan.zig lowers a bodyless effect_ir.Program, prefer rejecting helper graphs unless call_edges form an acyclic single-successor relation, because raw edge order otherwise invents execution order and recursion semantics.

fingerprint.learning: When adding helper-body lowering support, prefer ignoring ordinary line comments and budgeting scratch locals/instructions for max_function_params helper calls, because otherwise valid lowerAt inputs can fail at comptime.
evidence: Reviewed src/internal/helper_body_lowering.zig: isBodyIgnorable filters doc comments but not std.zig.Token.Tag.line_comment.

Validated repo with zig build test; coverage passes but does not exercise comment-bearing helper bodies or max-arity literal helper calls.
fingerprint.application: Add line_comment to the ignorable token set and budget helper-body scratch arrays for the supported maximum helper-argument lowering path.

fingerprint.learning: When helper-body lowering admits max_function_params helper calls, prefer sizing linear-body scratch arrays from the worst retained statement shape rather than fixed per-statement multipliers, because one max-arity helper call already emits 8 const locals plus call_helper and branching can demand two such helper-call lowerings in one statement.

zig build test --summary none -- --test-filter explicit-path passed after adding explicit-path regressions for ordinary // comments and max-arity helper calls.
fingerprint.application: For future public lowering helper-body work in shift, derive comptime scratch capacity from the supported lowering grammar's worst-case statement expansion and prove it with explicit-path regression tests before shipping.

fingerprint.learning: When public lowering sees an absolute sourceWithContent repo_path witness, prefer the owned-root check over plain absolute-path equality because otherwise downstream callers can bypass the ownership boundary with out-of-root mirrors; when deriving after-hook names for op declarations, preserve underscore runs to prevent distinct op names from collapsing onto one handler.
evidence: src/public_lowering.zig:597-600 gates absolute repo_path witnesses to owned roots before caller_hash bytes are trusted.
src/public_lowering.zig:2427-2445 pins distinct after-hook names for foo_bar, foo__bar, and _foo_bar at comptime.
zig build test && zig build downstream-public-lowering && zig build compile-fail all passed after the fix.
fingerprint.application: Prefer owned-root validation for absolute repo_path provenance and preserve underscores in synthesized after* names when hardening public lowering.

fingerprint.learning: When shift accepts file-backed validation for an absolute source path or nests a second runtime on the same thread, canonicalize the path back under package_root and re-enter repo-relative import resolution, and keep the active-runtime stack on a non-runtime allocator because that preserves fail-closed ownership boundaries and avoids allocator-traffic regressions while cleanup helpers switch back to the outer runtime correctly.
evidence: Validated with zig build, zig build test --summary none, zig build lint -- --max-warnings 0, zig build runtime-contract-suite, and zig build kernel-source-lowering-check after the fix.
Added regressions for rejecting non-owned absolute entry paths, allowing independent runtimes on one thread, and restoring cleanup ownership to the outer runtime after an inner runtime exits.
fingerprint.application: Use the same pattern for future shift runtime/lowering boundary fixes: fail closed by re-entering repo-relative ownership checks, and keep runtime bookkeeping off the runtime allocator while proving adjacent seams such as cleanup-stack restoration.

fingerprint.learning: When a shift review targets public lowering ownership or explicit IR validation, prefer proof hooks that use truly external fixture paths plus compile-fail operands before changing code, because .zig-cache fixtures can hide downstream provenance regressions and unchecked helper indexes can bypass structured rejection.

fingerprint.learning: When shift.lower(sourceWithContentAndImports(...)) lowers a caller-owned absolute entry that imports ../ helpers, require source_graph_embed.resolveImportPath to normalize the import path itself before joining because validateOwnedOpenRowAt rejects parent-relative helper hops and otherwise compile-time lowering can emit a GeneratedProgram that validate() later rejects with error.UnsupportedHelperGraph.

fingerprint.application: For shift public-lowering ownership fixes, require parent-relative helper imports for absolute caller-owned sources to fail at compile time and add a compile-fail fixture on sourceWithContentAndImports(...) so compile-time lowering and runtime validation stay fail-closed together.

fingerprint.learning: When shift replays legacy plan-backed durable sessions across plan schema upgrades, prefer preserving a pre-migration plan-hash witness from readPlanFile and comparing any current artifact plan against the migrated plan hash because legacy manifest hashes encode the old schema version and otherwise schema-only upgrades are misclassified as failed or rebuild_required.

fingerprint.learning: When shift.ir.compile lowers explicit IR into plan-only metadata, allow entry parameter_codecs there and keep the entry-argument rejection only on executable source-lowered entrypoints like shift.lower/shift.lowerAt.

fingerprint.learning: When shift hardens public lowering against malformed comptime Zig sources, prefer tokenizer-level fail-closed checks in source_graph_engine gated behind reject_malformed_statements instead of std.zig.Ast.parse at comptime, because Zig 0.15.2 cannot safely evaluate the AST parser in comptime lowering paths.

fingerprint.learning: When shift review comments target row-only ProgramPlan synthesis and validator invariants together, keep the fix fail-closed: bodyless value returns only admit an unambiguous unary parameter path, and missing payload/terminator mismatches belong in program-plan-check regressions.

fingerprint.learning: When shift returns authored bound programs that need activation, keep the runtime handler carrier in a prompt-keyed external activation stack instead of mutating handler_ctx inside the authored value, so const activate/deactivate stays source-compatible and runtime-safe.

fingerprint.learning: When shift.lower accepts caller-supplied source bytes, require non-owned caller roots to use exact absolute paths and require owned-root helper imports to mirror the checked-in repo bytes, because relative witnesses or helper overrides let lowering trust code outside the owned source set.

fingerprint.application: Use the same owned-root rule for future lowering or validation extensions: if a path denotes repo-owned code, every caller-supplied module on that lane must either resolve under package_root/package_root_alias and byte-match the repo, or the request must fail closed.

fingerprint.learning: When GeneratedProgramType.validate must detect drift for file-backed public lowering sources, prefer comparing each reachable on-disk module against a compile-time snapshot and returning SourceDrifted on mismatch, because runtime source_graph_engine body_lowering_supported is not a faithful proxy for the lowered helper-body subset and otherwise helper-body drift can pass validation.

fingerprint.application: Prefer compile-time module snapshots over runtime helper-body inference for future file-backed validation drift checks.

fingerprint.learning: When a durable plan migration starts from a shallow-copied ProgramPlan, prefer freeing the migrated plan owner rather than the pre-migration copy because migration can replace owned slices and otherwise allocator ownership is corrupted.

fingerprint.application: For future durable plan-schema migrations, keep cleanup attached to the post-migration owner and prove the error path with zig build durable-session-resume-check.

fingerprint.learning: When public explicit IR bodies enter ProgramPlan.validate, enforce function-local codec agreement for runtime-facing instructions and require return_value to be the final instruction in its block because otherwise malformed helper bodies can reach runtime unreachable paths or execute instructions after the logical return.

zig build program-plan-check --summary none -> ok
zig build test --summary none -- --test-filter "explicit ir run result outputs stay scoped to the entry function" -> ok
fingerprint.application: Use the validator seam in src/internal/program_plan.zig for future explicit-IR repairs, then prove public output behavior through test/open_row_lowering_test.zig when RunResult.outputs changes.

fingerprint.learning: When source-graph flattening feeds entry_symbol lowering, prefer expanding imported helper edges only from the entry-reachable function subgraph because dead helper imports otherwise raise MissingImport before reachableFunctions can prune them.

zig build test --summary none -- --test-filter "explicit-path" passed after the fix
fingerprint.application: In source_graph_embed-style flatteners, collect local module structure first and add cross-file helper edges lazily from reachable callers instead of recursing over every helper_use in the module.

fingerprint.learning: When shift gates helper-import escape compile-fail fixtures or resolves helper imports from source graphs, prefer probing symlink support in the actual fixture directory and decoding Zig string-literal escapes before helper-path checks because cache-root probes and raw escape text otherwise misclassify host support or report MissingImport for valid helper modules.

zig build test --summary none -- --test-filter explicit-path
zig build compile-fail --summary none
fingerprint.application: Probe on the same filesystem as the real symlink destination, and decode helper import paths before .zig-suffix checks or validation joins to avoid host-dependent gating and false helper misses.

fingerprint.learning: When shift lowers or validates absolute caller-owned helper graphs, prefer carrying the first admitted helper-tree root across every recursive import edge because re-anchoring each hop to the current module path lets helpers escape into sibling trees.
evidence: source_graph_embed.zig stores absolute_entry_tree_root per collected module and rejects resolved absolute imports that leave that root.
src/public_lowering.zig mirrors absolute_entry_tree_root through owned validation and fails transitive escapes closed as error.UnsupportedHelperGraph.
fingerprint.application: Prefer one admitted-tree root per absolute helper branch and reuse it for all later path resolutions instead of re-deriving admissibility from the current module alone.

fingerprint.learning: When shift lowers or validates absolute caller-owned helper imports, prefer one normalized absoluteOwnedImportBoundary shared by lowering and validation, because deriving the admitted tree from raw segments or dirname(resolved_path) wrongly rejects shared helper subtrees and normalized sibling-tree imports.
evidence: Implemented source_graph_embed.absoluteOwnedImportBoundary and switched source_graph_embed.resolveImportPath plus public_lowering.resolveOwnedValidationImportPathAlloc to anchor zero-parent imports to the entry directory and one-parent imports to the sibling directory.

fingerprint.application: Use the shared normalized boundary helper for future absolute caller-owned helper graph fixes instead of re-encoding path-tree rules at individual call sites.

fingerprint.learning: When public lowering executes helper or explicit-IR loops with after* handlers, prefer continuation-style after unwind instead of an instruction-count-bounded after stack, because repeated dynamic call_op resumes can otherwise hit ProgramContractViolation before the final answer is produced.
evidence: src/public_lowering.zig now recurses from resumed call_op sites and applies after* on unwind instead of pushing op ids into a function.instruction_count-sized array.
A focused root zig test filtered to 'loop resumes' passes both the raw executor regression and a CompileIr(...).run(...) regression that previously overflowed the bounded after stack.
fingerprint.application: Use continuation-shaped unwind for future lowered runtime resume bookkeeping whenever dynamic op count can exceed the static instruction span.

fingerprint.learning: When public-lowering validation traverses imported helper modules, prefer recursing through imported helper uses even when the caller helper lacks an effect parameter, because otherwise nested pure helper drift escapes file-backed and snapshot validation.

zig build test --summary none -- --test-filter 'file-backed validation detects drift in nested imported pure helper chains'

fingerprint.learning: When shift public lowering validates file-backed helper imports, track per-module reachable callers from the requested symbol and expand imported helper modules only for newly reachable helper uses; walking every helper_use in the module turns entry validation into module-wide validation.
evidence: src/public_lowering.zig now stores helper_edges plus reachable_functions/expanded_helper_uses in ValidationModule and only descends imports from reachable callers.
Regression proof: zig build test --summary none -- --test-filter "review regression".
fingerprint.application: For future public-lowering validation fixes, preserve entry-scoped reachability when traversing imported helper graphs instead of validating unrelated dead helpers.

fingerprint.learning: When shift ownership checks compare caller-visible paths, treat Windows absolute paths as case-insensitive but still keep repo-relative matching case-sensitive, and route owned-path registry lookups through the same separator-aware comparator so backslash repo paths remain owned.

source_graph_embed.zig applies the same Windows case-folding policy to repoRelativeAbsolutePath and registryContainsLine.

Proof: zig build test --summary none -- --test-filter 'windows ownership portability'; zig build compile-fail --summary none; zig build downstream-public-lowering --summary none; zig build lint --summary none -- --max-warnings 0.
fingerprint.application: Use the existing path comparator as the single ownership admission rule for both absolute-root checks and repo-path registry membership; add synthetic Windows-path tests before widening any ownership helper changes.

fingerprint.learning: When shift durable restore cannot load a required artifact or plan sidecar after legacy fallback, prefer returning rebuild_required instead of failed because replay equivalence cannot be proven.

fingerprint.learning: When shift public lowering accepts an absolute repo-owned witness where repo_path == caller_file, require caller_source to mirror the embedded repo bytes under the owned root, because path equality plus caller_hash alone otherwise reopens a compile-vs-validate split.

fingerprint.learning: When seeded public_lowering review failures stop at expected type expression found brace, prefer inspecting the tail of src/public_lowering.zig for stray non-Zig artifacts before editing compile-fail expectations, because parser poison can mask whether downstream fixtures are actually stale.

fingerprint.learning: When shift validates caller-owned lowering built from absolute sourceWithContent paths, prefer reading the live absolute file during validate because replaying only the embedded snapshot hides on-disk drift from GeneratedProgramType.validate.

fingerprint.application: Use live on-disk reads for absolute external caller-owned validation paths, and keep schema-2 durable manifests plan_schema_version null so legacy plan-file migrators can infer the real stored schema instead of forcing rebuild_required.

fingerprint.learning: When build.zig prepares external fixture files or symlinks for public-lowering or compile-fail lanes, prefer custom std.fs-backed build steps over shell-based sh/mkdir/rm/ln helpers because the fixture graph must stay portable on hosts without a POSIX userland, and deep-copy writer payload bytes before exposing handler outputs to prevent dangling or mutated slices after run returns.
evidence: zig build compile-fail --summary none -- --test-filter escape -> ok

fingerprint.learning: When a cross-file open-row helper is meant to exercise public lowering, keep the helper body in the retained straight-line subset and preserve the advertised writer side effects in that helper module, because replacing it with an unsupported loop body both breaks kernel-source-lowering-check at comptime and drops the runtime transcript contract.

fingerprint.application: Use this when editing cross-file helper fixtures in shift: mirror the supported same-file helper pattern unless the change is explicitly extending helper-body lowering with its own proof lane.

fingerprint.learning: When shift clones legacy durable plan files for replay migration, prefer copying the full persisted FunctionPlan and only re-owning symbol_name because field-by-field reconstruction drops value_codec, parameter_count, and entry_block and corrupts replay/hash compatibility.

zig build test --summary none -- --test-filter preserves legacy function metadata

fingerprint.learning: When body-bearing explicit IR lowers helper calls into ProgramPlan, prefer validating that each FunctionBody local-codec prefix matches the declared parameter_codecs and unwind caller after-handlers when a helper returns terminal control, because otherwise malformed helper signatures are accepted and helper terminals skip pending after* transforms.

fingerprint.learning: When shift runtime frame lookup must disambiguate active prompts that reuse one token, prefer scanning runtime_previous_for_token for an exact prompt pointer match and then falling back to the current token head, because prompt values are token-only and copyable and a pointer-only policy breaks existing binding-packet flows while a head-only policy routes explicit token collisions to the wrong frame.
evidence: Implemented in src/frontend.zig findFrame with prompt-identity traversal plus a token-head fallback, and added a same-runtime Prompt.initWithToken collision regression.

fingerprint.application: Use identity-first plus compatibility fallback whenever shift needs to separate same-token runtime frames without introducing a new stable prompt identity field.

fingerprint.learning: When shift synthesizes public-lowering after-hook names from user op names, prefer sizing the comptime buffer to 5 + op_name.len instead of a fixed cap, because otherwise valid long op names fail compilation with an internal bounds error.

fingerprint.application: Use the same exact-size-buffer pattern for future comptime name-synthesis helpers, and pair it with a focused compile-only root-test filter when the broad branch test step is red for unrelated reasons.
