General Options: -p, --prefix [path] Where to install files (default: zig-out) --prefix-lib-dir [path] Where to install libraries --prefix-exe-dir [path] Where to install executables --prefix-include-dir [path] Where to install C header files --release[=mode] Request release mode, optionally specifying a preferred optimization mode: fast, safe, small -fdarling, -fno-darling Integration with system-installed Darling to execute macOS programs on Linux hosts (default: no) -fqemu, -fno-qemu Integration with system-installed QEMU to execute foreign-architecture programs on Linux hosts (default: no) --libc-runtimes [path] Enhances QEMU integration by providing dynamic libc (e.g. glibc or musl) built for multiple foreign architectures, allowing execution of non-native programs that link with libc. -frosetta, -fno-rosetta Rely on Rosetta to execute x86_64 programs on ARM64 macOS hosts. (default: no) -fwasmtime, -fno-wasmtime Integration with system-installed wasmtime to execute WASI binaries. (default: no) -fwine, -fno-wine Integration with system-installed Wine to execute Windows programs on Linux hosts. (default: no) -h, --help Print this help and exit -l, --list-steps Print available steps --verbose Print commands before executing them --color [auto|off|on] Enable or disable colored error messages --prominent-compile-errors Buffer compile errors and display at end --summary [mode] Control the printing of the build summary all Print the build summary in its entirety new Omit cached steps failures (Default) Only print failed steps none Do not print the build summary -j<N> Limit concurrent jobs (default is to use all CPU cores) --maxrss <bytes> Limit memory usage (default is to use available memory) --skip-oom-steps Instead of failing, skip steps that would exceed --maxrss --fetch[=mode] Fetch dependency tree (optionally choose laziness) and exit needed (Default) Lazy dependencies are fetched as needed all Lazy dependencies are always fetched --watch Continuously rebuild when source files are modified --debounce <ms> Delay before rebuilding after changed file detected --webui[=ip] Enable the web interface on the given IP address --fuzz Continuously search for unit test failures (implies '--webui') --time-report Force full rebuild and provide detailed information on compilation time of Zig source code (implies '--webui') -fincremental Enable incremental compilation -fno-incremental Disable incremental compilation Project-Specific Options: -Dtarget=[string] The CPU architecture, OS, and ABI to build for -Dcpu=[string] Target CPU features to add or subtract -Dofmt=[string] Target object format -Ddynamic-linker=[string] Path to interpreter on the target system -Doptimize=[enum] Prioritize performance, safety, or binary size Supported Values: Debug ReleaseSafe ReleaseFast ReleaseSmall System Integration Options: --search-prefix [path] Add a path to look for binaries, libraries, headers --sysroot [path] Set the system root directory (usually /) --libc [file] Provide a file which specifies libc paths --system [pkgdir] Disable package fetching; enable all integrations -fsys=[name] Enable a system integration -fno-sys=[name] Disable a system integration Available System Integrations: Enabled: (none) - Advanced Options: -freference-trace[=num] How many lines of reference trace should be shown per compile error -fno-reference-trace Disable reference trace -fallow-so-scripts Allows .so files to be GNU ld scripts -fno-allow-so-scripts (default) .so files must be ELF files --build-file [file] Override path to build.zig --cache-dir [path] Override path to local Zig cache directory --global-cache-dir [path] Override path to global Zig cache directory --zig-lib-dir [arg] Override path to Zig lib directory --build-runner [file] Override path to build runner --seed [integer] For shuffling dependency traversal order (default: random) --build-id[=style] At a minor link-time expense, embeds a build ID in binaries fast 8-byte non-cryptographic hash (COFF, ELF, WASM) sha1, tree 20-byte cryptographic hash (ELF, WASM) md5 16-byte cryptographic hash (ELF) uuid 16-byte random UUID (ELF, WASM) 0x[hexstring] Constant ID, maximum 32 bytes (ELF, WASM) none (default) No build ID --debug-log [scope] Enable debugging the compiler --debug-pkg-config Fail if unknown pkg-config flags encountered --debug-rt Debug compiler runtime libraries --verbose-link Enable compiler debug output for linking --verbose-air Enable compiler debug output for Zig AIR --verbose-llvm-ir[=file] Enable compiler debug output for LLVM IR --verbose-llvm-bc=[file] Enable compiler debug output for LLVM BC --verbose-cimport Enable compiler debug output for C imports --verbose-cc Enable compiler debug output for C compilation --verbose-llvm-cpu-features Enable compiler debug output for LLVM CPU features after the build.zig fallback was added
fingerprint.application: Use a dual-path registry in build.zig and keep findFrame() fail-closed on identity miss when same-token prompts can be copied or reused.

fingerprint.learning: When shift runtime frame lookup runs under an active runtime and multiple prompt values reuse one token, require an exact prompt_identity match and return null on miss instead of falling back to the runtime token head, because copied or reused tokens that were never pushed must raise MissingPrompt rather than dispatching to another frame.
evidence: src/frontend.zig findFrame now returns null after exhausting runtime_previous_for_token on prompt_identity miss.
A same-token orphan-prompt regression test now expects findFrame(...) == null and transform(...) == error.MissingPrompt while another frame with that token is active.
zig build frontend-internal-check passed on April 7, 2026 after the fix.
fingerprint.application: For future shift prompt-token collision work, treat token equality as a lookup bucket only and keep active-runtime dispatch identity-exact at the final selection step.

fingerprint.learning: When shift needs build_options.repo_zig_paths outside a live Git checkout, prefer a committed git-derived repo_zig_paths.txt snapshot over failing the build graph or scanning the filesystem, because release archives must still build while ownership must not widen to untracked local .zig files.
evidence: build.zig now returns readCommittedRepoZigPathRegistry(b) when git ls-files is unavailable or exits non-zero.
repo_zig_paths.txt matched git ls-files -- '*.zig' exactly on April 7, 2026.
A temporary copy of the repo without .git completed zig build --help successfully after adding repo_zig_paths.txt.
fingerprint.application: For future shift ownership-registry changes, keep the committed registry as the archive fallback artifact and verify it against tracked Git paths before shipping.

fingerprint.learning: When shift build-graph ownership depends on tracked repo Zig paths, keep git ls-files as the live-checkout source but fall back to a committed registry file for archive or no-git builds, and prove the fallback with a .git-free zig build --help lane.
evidence: build.zig repoZigPathRegistry now returns readCommittedRepoZigPathRegistry when collectTrackedRepoZigPaths cannot use git.
Validated with zig build frontend-internal-check, zig build lint -- --max-warnings 0, and zig build --help from /tmp/shift-review-archive-fast copied without .git and caches excluded.
fingerprint.application: For future build-graph ownership registries in shift, keep the fail-closed tracked-file semantics for live repos while preserving archive portability with a versioned committed fallback and a no-.git proof lane.

fingerprint.learning: Keep prompt-pointer identity disambiguation explicit in sealed-engine and generated-family runners; prompt_token alone is not sufficient for frame lookup when multiple frames share a token.
evidence: frontend.findFrame compares prompt_identity for same-token disambiguation in src/frontend.zig:953-970.
This turn replaced synthetic PromptType reconstruction with direct prompt_identity threading in src/effect/algebraic.zig and src/effect/generated_family.zig.
fingerprint.application: When touching prompt-carrier plumbing, preserve the actual prompt pointer at the boundary and verify with targeted frontend/internal proof lanes before widening the change.

fingerprint.learning: When shift sealed-engine handlers stop reconstructing prompts from tokens, make every binding in the algebraic/generated-family engine reuse the same live instance-prompt pointer and keep active-runtime frame lookup falling back to compat on miss, because token-only cloned binding prompts otherwise cause false MissingPrompt failures on lexical, abort, and nested-runtime paths.
evidence: src/effect/algebraic.zig and src/effect/generated_family.zig require explicit *const anyopaque prompt_identity typing before the anytype config literal reaches runWithSealedEngine.

src/internal/algebraic_engine.zig must initialize bindings from the live instance prompt and route perform/abort/program prompt references through that shared identity; otherwise lexical-with and runtime-contract lanes fail in frontend.abortWithContext with MissingPrompt.

Proof lanes that passed after the fix: zig build frontend-internal-check --summary none -- --test-filter "findFrame falls back to compat frames when an active inner runtime misses an outer prompt|prompt-token collisions|MissingPrompt|same-token"; zig build runtime-contract-suite --summary none; zig build lexical-with-suite --summary none.
fingerprint.application: Preserve runtime-local findFrame priority, fall back to compat only after a runtime miss, and treat prompt identity as an end-to-end invariant across instance prompt, binding prompt, authored-program prompt, and frontend frame lookup to avoid reintroducing false MissingPrompt regressions.

fingerprint.learning: When shift durable restore reads persisted plan files across schema upgrades, prefer probing top-level and nested plan schema with ignore_unknown_fields before strict v4 PlanFile parsing because legacy instruction fields otherwise raise UnknownField too early and restore misclassifies rebuildable sessions as failed.
evidence: src/durable.zig readPlanFile schema probe before strict PlanFile parse
zig build test --summary none -- --test-filter readPlanFile\ rejects
zig build durable-session-resume-check --summary none
fingerprint.application: Keep legacy-schema discrimination at the readPlanFile boundary and preserve strict current-schema parsing only after the probe passes.

fingerprint.learning: When explicit-IR helper calls can bubble terminal control across a caller/callee codec boundary, prefer rejecting the plan in ProgramPlan.validate and checking helper-terminal tags at runtime, because otherwise malformed plans can reach decode unreachable instead of failing closed.
evidence: src/internal/program_plan.zig fixed-point terminal reachability over helper call graph inside ProgramPlan.validate
src/public_lowering.zig runtimeValueMatchesCodec guard on helper terminal propagation
zig build program-plan-check --summary none; zig build test --summary none -- --test-filter 'helper terminal'; zig build lint -- --max-warnings 0
fingerprint.application: Use this for explicit-IR/runtime plan fixes where helper-terminal codec agreement must hold transitively across helper subgraphs.

fingerprint.learning: When helper-level provenance synthesis can auto-satisfy canonical owned-root absolute callers, prefer basename-only regression fixtures for no-content ownership-negative cases so the fail-closed branch stays isolated.
evidence: src/public_lowering.zig:809-831 now backfills caller_source/caller_hash for owned-root absolute callers
test/compile_fail/source_ownership_relative_no_content_witness_fails.zig:4-18 now uses basename-only open_row_state_writer.zig to keep the no-content branch covered
fingerprint.application: Use basename-only fixtures for ownership-negative cases whenever helper-level provenance can auto-satisfy canonical owned-root callers.

fingerprint.learning: When shift tracks nested active runtimes on one thread, guard teardown before mutating the active-runtime registry: return RuntimeBusy from a checked shutdown path on mismatched end order, and keep the unchecked wrapper fail-closed so release builds cannot silently pop the wrong runtime.
evidence: src/lowered_machine.zig adds endExecutionChecked plus a regression that proves activeRuntime stays on the inner runtime after a rejected outer shutdown.
zig build test
zig build lint -- --max-warnings 0
fingerprint.application: Use a checked teardown API whenever nested execution state is stack-shaped, and make unchecked teardown delegate to it so release-mode misuse turns into an explicit failure instead of registry corruption.

fingerprint.learning: When shift durable restore depends on events.jsonl, treat a missing events sidecar as rebuild_required and create parent directories in the shared durable write helpers so first-run nested store paths persist without caller-side makePath workarounds.
evidence: src/durable.zig:120-126 adds event-sidecar rebuild classification and src/durable.zig:589-670 makes all durable writes ensure parent directories first.

test/durable_session_test.zig:217-230, 291-325, and 561-582 lock the missing-events and fresh-nested-path cases; zig build durable-session-resume-check --summary none and zig build lint -- --max-warnings 0 both passed.
fingerprint.application: For future durable sidecars, keep degraded restore classification symmetric across manifest/plan/artifact/events and centralize first-write path creation in shared file writers instead of requiring callers or demos to pre-create directories.

fingerprint.learning: When validating helper codec compatibility, prefer a CFG-aware reachability check over the current function-wide instruction scan, because an unreachable terminal-only block can otherwise mark the callee terminal-reachable and reject a mismatched caller codec.
evidence: src/internal/program_plan.zig:187-215 scans all instructions in each function to set terminal_reachability
src/internal/program_plan.zig:231-238 rejects helper calls when terminal_reachability is true and callee.value_codec differs
test/program_plan_review_regression_test.zig:493-577 exercises the terminal-control mismatch lane
fingerprint.application: Use a dead-block fixture to distinguish true live terminal reachability from accidental function-wide terminal reachability before relying on this guard.

fingerprint.learning: When ProgramPlan rules depend on whether helper terminal control can escape, prefer deriving reachability from each function entry block and only rejecting reachable caller sites, because scanning unreachable terminal-only blocks rejects valid explicit-IR plans.
evidence: src/internal/program_plan.zig: validate now marks reachable_blocks from entry_block before terminal_reachability and only applies the helper codec mismatch rejection when the caller block is reachable
test/program_plan_review_regression_test.zig: the reachable terminal escape regression still fails, while a helper with an unreachable abort block now validates
fingerprint.application: Apply entry-scoped block reachability to future validation checks that reason about terminal control or other path-sensitive helper effects, to avoid dead blocks poisoning whole-function verdicts.

fingerprint.learning: When closing a merged shift PR with gh pr merge --squash --delete-branch, run git fetch --prune origin after the merge, because the local checkout may already be on main while stale remote-tracking branch refs persist until prune.
evidence: gh pr merge 11 --squash --delete-branch fast-forwarded local checkout from 8823951 to bd411c1 and left repo on main.
git branch -a still showed origin/feature/binding-packet-portability before prune, and git fetch --prune origin removed it.
fingerprint.application: Use status plus prune as the final cleanup proof in future fin closeouts for this repo.

fingerprint.learning: When shift handled-effect benchmarks show large fixed tax but zprof shows raw and effect writer/resource lanes have nearly identical allocation counts and bytes, optimize runtime/control bookkeeping first and treat the remaining hotspot as CPU overhead rather than allocator volume.
evidence: zig build bench-state-effect dropped raw_reset_only from about 293774375ns median per 50000 iters before the runtime-stack change to 1866417ns after it.

zig build zprof-hotspots showed writer_effect_batch64 alloc_bytes=3136160 alloc_count=8001 versus writer_raw_batch64 alloc_bytes=3136000 alloc_count=8000, and resource_effect_32 alloc_bytes=896160 alloc_count=4001 versus resource_raw_32 alloc_bytes=896000 alloc_count=4000.
fingerprint.application: For the next shift perf pass, use benchmark surfaces to find fixed tax, use zprof to rule allocator volume in or out, and switch to CPU/control profiling only after allocator counters stay flat.

fingerprint.learning: When shift replaces an allocator-backed active-runtime registry with a threadlocal fast path, keep the common-case stack inline but spill overflow frames onto a non-runtime allocator so >256 nested executions preserve prior nesting semantics without reintroducing benchmark-path allocation churn.
evidence: src/lowered_machine.zig now keeps the first 256 active runtimes in threadlocal storage and spills deeper nesting through ActiveRuntimeOverflowEntry on std.heap.page_allocator while preserving RuntimeBusy teardown checks.

Validated with zig build test --summary none, zig build lint -- --max-warnings 0, zig build bench-runtime-backends --summary none, and zig build zprof-hotspots --summary none after adding the >256 nesting regression.
fingerprint.application: Use the same hybrid fast-path-plus-overflow pattern for future shift runtime bookkeeping changes whenever a perf-motivated fixed-cap structure would otherwise narrow a previously allocator-limited public contract.

fingerprint.learning: When shift zprof compares raw and effect allocator volume, prefer warming each effect lane once and resetting the profiler before measured iterations because one-time runtime or handler bootstrap otherwise looks like steady-state effect allocation overhead.
evidence: bench/zprof_hotspots.zig now runs one warmup handle for writer/resource effect lanes, frees any warmup output, and calls profiler.reset() before the measured loop while keeping runtime teardown after printing.

zig build zprof-hotspots --summary none now reports exact parity for writer/resource raw vs effect lanes: writer alloc_bytes=3136000 alloc_count=8000 free_count=8000 and resource alloc_bytes=896000 alloc_count=4000 free_count=4000 on both sides.
fingerprint.application: For future shift allocator profiles, exclude fixed handler bootstrap with an explicit warmup-plus-reset step before using raw-vs-effect counter deltas to choose the next optimization target.

fingerprint.learning: When a shift profiling-only build step depends on an external package, prefer marking that package lazy in build.zig.zon and resolving it with std.Build.lazyDependency at the step site because eager dependency resolution makes routine non-profiling builds depend on optional tooling.
evidence: build.zig.zon now marks zprof with .lazy = true, and build.zig resolves it only inside the zprof-hotspots step via b.lazyDependency("zprof", .{ .target = target, .optimize = bench_optimize }).
zig build test --summary none, zig build zprof-hotspots --summary none, and zig build lint -- --max-warnings 0 all passed after the change.
fingerprint.application: For future shift profiling or dev-only build surfaces, keep optional tooling dependencies step-local so core build/test/lint paths do not couple to them at graph construction time.

fingerprint.learning: When a shift replay-era benchmark needs resumptive raw or algebraic transform lanes, encode those lanes as explicit program builders like transformProgram(...) or ctx.performProgram(...) instead of body-style compute thunks, because suspended transform bodies can surface FrontendSuspend and break documented benchmark commands.
evidence: bench/algebraic_builder_decompose_bench.zig now runs its raw lane through shift.transformProgram(...) and its configured full-path lane through ctx.performProgram(...).
zig build bench-family-builder-decompose --summary none passed on April 8, 2026 after the change and printed raw_transform, configured_run_only, and configured_transform samples.
zig build test --summary none -- --test-filter 'transform program resumes and observes final answer' passed on April 8, 2026.
fingerprint.application: For future shift benchmark-facade migrations, treat resumptive benchmark lanes as explicit-program contracts and verify the documented build step after any prompt-support or facade rewrite.

fingerprint.learning: When resetting a tracked st plan on shift with st 0.1.3, prefer snapshotting first, deleting stale items in reverse dependency order, and rebuilding the campaign explicitly, because import-plan appends and forward removals fail on dependency checks.
evidence: st 0.1.3 lacks newer claim/import-orchplan surface and only exposes import-plan plus remove/set-* mutation commands
a temporary-file probe showed import-plan appended new items instead of replacing the existing inventory
forward deletes failed with UnknownDependency until items were removed from leaves back using jq reverse order
fingerprint.application: Use export plus reverse-order remove before future campaign pivots, then regenerate plan_sync and mirror update_plan from the rebuilt durable graph.

fingerprint.learning: When a Zig package split needs multiple same-package public modules that consumers will import together, prefer one shared owner module plus thin wrapper modules rather than letting each public module own overlapping source files.
evidence: zig build size-check initially failed with repeated exists: cannot open `exists' (No such file or directory) in: cannot open `in' (No such file or directory) modules: cannot open `modules' (No such file or directory) shift: cannot open `shift' (No such file or directory) and: cannot open `and' (No such file or directory) shift_vm: cannot open `shift_vm' (No such file or directory) / ownership errors while building the new root/module split.
The fix was to introduce src/shift_shared.zig and route shift, shift_compile, and shift_vm through that shared owner, then cut remaining root back-edges in effect/* and internal/algebraic_engine.zig.
After the shared-owner refactor, zig build size-check, zig build public-root-contract-snapshot-check, zig build public-error-api-ban, and zig build readme-contract all passed.
fingerprint.application: Before changing root exports in future same-package splits, design and land the shared owner module first, then layer wrapper entrypoints on top and only then flip the public contract proofs.

fingerprint.learning: When a root hard-cut removes public exports in a same-package Zig repo but internal proof fixtures still need the old combined surface, prefer routing those internal consumers through one shared internal module in build.zig rather than restoring the old public root, because that preserves the fail-closed public contract while keeping internal proof lanes compiling.
evidence: After the root cut, zig build kernel-parity-check and zig build durable-session-resume-check failed on missing shift.run/shift.durable/shift.interpreter members from src/root.zig.

Rewiring internal build consumers to import shift_shared under the local name shift made zig build kernel-parity-check and zig build durable-session-resume-check pass again without restoring the removed public root exports.
The real public root proof lane still passed afterward via zig build size-check and zig build public-root-contract-snapshot-check.
fingerprint.application: For future split waves in shift, keep public-root checks on src/root.zig and move internal proof/example consumers by build wiring first before editing dozens of fixtures.

fingerprint.learning: When proving Zig native-to-WASM parity for a same-package runtime surface, prefer one shared runner source compiled for both native and wasm32-wasi plus a wasmtime diff script, because that turns portability claims into exact-output evidence instead of target-specific narratives.
evidence: Installed wasmtime and proved the same ArtifactV1 state-writer runner through zig build artifact-vm-wasm-parity-check after fixing a wasm-only u64-to-usize decode cast in src/agent_vm_artifact.zig.
The parity lane used one shared runner source in tools/artifact_vm_state_writer_runner.zig and compared native output against wasmtime output in test/artifact_vm_wasm_parity/run.sh.
fingerprint.application: For future shift portability work, add the shared runner and wasmtime diff lane before claiming wasm execution parity on a new runtime surface.

fingerprint.learning: When strict zlinter conflicts with a retained public compatibility alias like shift.ir, keep the API surface and scope the exception to a single declaration with an inline zlinter disable plus doc comment instead of weakening repo-wide lint coverage.
evidence: src/shift_shared.zig keeps the public shift.ir alias and uses a one-line declaration_naming disable on that declaration only.
zig build lint -- --max-warnings 0 passed after the scoped suppression and the remaining runtime/test/tool warnings were fixed directly.
fingerprint.application: For future retained compatibility aliases that violate mechanical naming rules, require a documented per-declaration suppression and full strict-lint proof instead of file exclusions or semantic renames.

fingerprint.learning: When ArtifactV1 VM runs explicit capability manifests or string-returning host ops, prefer resolving capability_id/op_id by manifest order plus declared ids and cloning host strings into an execution arena because assuming id==requirement_index or borrowing response-owned slices will break valid manifests and leave dangling ProgramValue strings after response deinit.
evidence: src/artifact_vm_runtime.zig: runArtifact now uses an ArenaAllocator for execution values; callHostOp resolves capability/op ids from the manifest slice and clones string payloads via value_allocator.
test/artifact_vm_runtime_test.zig: explicit non-zero capability/op id coverage and later-op terminal string coverage now exercise the failure modes directly.
zig build artifact-v1-api-check artifact-vm-runtime-check bundle-envelope-v1-check
fingerprint.application: For future artifact/bundle/runtime review fixes, treat capability-id equality and borrowed host strings as fail-closed boundary invariants and prove them with direct runtime regression tests.

fingerprint.learning: When an agent-VM v1 surface advertises future capability kinds that the runtime cannot actually construct or consume, prefer contracting code and docs to the implemented tool-only boundary and encoding requirement-to-capability ids in the artifact because truthful fail-closed contracts and explicit id mapping prevent misleading APIs and manifest-order routing bugs.
evidence: src/host_adapter_v1.zig and docs/host_adapter_v1_contract.md now define a tool-only HostAdapterV1 v1 surface instead of exposing model/durable variants without payload/result shapes.

src/agent_vm_artifact.zig now encodes per-requirement capability ids in the requirement table, and src/artifact_vm_runtime.zig resolves runtime dispatch by declared capability_id rather than manifest row position.
zig build artifact-v1-api-check artifact-vm-runtime-check host-adapter-conformance-check agent-vm-spec-contract && zig build && zig build test --summary none && zig build lint -- --max-warnings 0
fingerprint.application: For future review-driven API hardening in shift, treat unsupported future-facing enums and doc-only promises as fail-closed contraction candidates, then add direct regression tests that permute manifest order or contract anchors.

fingerprint.learning: When touching agent-vm artifact/runtime manifest routing, run the dedicated artifact-vm proof lane explicitly; zig build test can stay green while artifact-vm-runtime-check fails on manifest/runtime contract mismatches.
evidence: build.zig defines artifact-vm-runtime-check as a separate step from the default test step.
2026-04-09: zig build test -- --max-warnings 0 passed while zig build artifact-vm-runtime-check failed in test 'ArtifactV1 runtime decodes terminal string results for later requirement ops'.
fingerprint.application: For agent-vm changes under src/agent_vm_artifact.zig, src/artifact_vm_runtime.zig, src/bundle_envelope_v1.zig, or tools/artifact_vm_state_writer_runner.zig, treat zig build artifact-vm-runtime-check as a required gate in addition to the default test/lint lanes.

fingerprint.learning: When shift artifact/bundle review remediation touches public host or manifest boundaries, prefer direct proof lanes for runner build, bundle exact-build rejection, host integer failure behavior, and custom capability-plan alignment because the main test suite can miss dead parity tooling and fail-open host boundary bugs.
evidence: tools/artifact_vm_state_writer_runner.zig needed stale .ok -> .success fixes to restore zig build artifact-vm-runner and artifact-vm-wasm-parity-check.

fingerprint.learning: When HostAdapterV1 promises borrowed payload trees and typed provider failures, keep DataValueV1 borrowed/container storage const-friendly and synthesize .failed provider_failure results for thrown dispatch errors, because otherwise ordinary backend faults surface as ProgramContractViolation and immutable Zig literals require @constCast or unnecessary allocation.

fingerprint.learning: When ArtifactV1 exact-build fingerprints start from git-tracked repo Zig paths and build.zig wires a new unstaged module, harvest .zig path literals from build() in build.zig because otherwise two different executables can share the same exact-build identity; keep the supplement scoped to build() to avoid hashing unrelated scratch files.

zig build lint -- --max-warnings 0
zig build artifact-v1-api-check
zig build test

fingerprint.learning: When shift's hard lint gate uses zlinter in this repo, pass the tracked Zig file registry as explicit builder.addPaths(.include=...) inputs instead of relying on recursive cwd walking, because zlinter's walkDirectory path-resolution path can panic on allocator leaks and otherwise block lint closure.

fingerprint.application: Use explicit registry-backed include paths for future shift lint wiring changes so lint stays fail-closed without depending on zlinter recursive scans.

fingerprint.learning: When ArtifactV1 decode revalidates a rebuilt ProgramPlan, prefer threading the caller allocator through ArtifactV1.validate instead of falling back to std.heap.page_allocator, because allocator-bounded hosts and allocation-failure proofs need decode and validate to stay inside the caller's budget.

fingerprint.application: For future shift artifact decode or import paths that rebuild temporary plans, keep validation allocations caller-owned and add an allocation-failure regression whenever a new validation clone path is introduced.

fingerprint.learning: When build.zig needs repo Zig paths for both lint inputs and exact-build identity, prefer one shared allocator-backed fallback collector because separate git-only and fallback implementations drift into package/tarball failures and stale deleted-file entries.

zig test build.zig --test-filter 'repo Zig path registry'
zig build lint -- --max-warnings 0
fingerprint.application: Use the shared collector before adding or changing build-time consumers of tracked Zig paths so non-git checkouts and deleted tracked files stay handled consistently.

fingerprint.learning: When ArtifactV1 parity dispatchers compile with derived capabilities, generated tool.after callbacks still unwind through the host; keep HostAdapterV1 ctx nullable for stateless adapters and make parity hosts echo after* arguments so native/WASM proof lanes do not depend on undefined ctx placeholders or missing after handlers.

fingerprint.learning: When an ArtifactV1 compile/runtime path needs default after-hook or output parity, encode authored after-hook presence explicitly in ProgramPlan/ArtifactV1 and require HostAdapterV1.collectOutputsFn for declared outputs, because inferring tool.after from codec shape or reading values from output_table will overstate the contract and silently change execution semantics.

fingerprint.learning: When ArtifactV1 completion can return a typed .failed result before value handoff and after-hooks must preserve HostAdapterV1 identity, prefer an unconditional defer for owned RuntimeValue cleanup and store the originating tool.call call_id in the unwind frame, because errdefer will not run on normal union returns and regenerating call_id breaks tool.after contract continuity.

fingerprint.learning: When shift's hard lint gate uses zlinter with an explicit tracked-file include registry, filter repo-specific exceptions out of the include list instead of passing builder excludes, because zlinter's exclude-index construction can panic before it reports warnings.

fingerprint.learning: When closing shift PR review comments against a live feature branch, inspect current HEAD and the targeted regression tests before patching, because the commented failure modes may already be remediated and blindly re-patching can add redundant churn.

fingerprint.learning: When decoding or validating ProgramPlan ops in shift, prefer checking that each op index falls inside the first_op..op_count span of its serialized requirement_index, because otherwise a forged requirement_index can misbind capability dispatch even when the plan passes basic range validation.

fingerprint.learning: When proving shift's single-front package boundary inside a review loop, mirror only the published package paths into generated downstream consumers and keep scratch roots under /tmp, because symlinking the full repo or writing fixtures outside reviewer-writable roots can turn native review rounds into harness failures instead of real publication bugs.

fingerprint.learning: When shift proves a single-front package boundary with nested downstream fixture builds, keep scratch roots under a portable temp directory and give each fixture its own writable global cache seeded only with the parent package cache, because native review sandboxes may forbid writes to the parent global cache and full-cache cloning can turn the proof lane into cleanup debt or broken compiler_rt artifacts.

fingerprint.application: When touching build.zig test frontends, prefer compile-time test filters plus step-scoped post-double-dash parsing over RunStep argv forwarding so local iteration stays correct and fail-closed without breaking other build steps.

fingerprint.learning: When a build option like -Dtest-suites only applies to one step, gate validation on explicit step detection before parsing the option so unrelated invocations stay fail-open for other steps and only the target step rejects bad input.
evidence: build.zig: buildInvocationRequestsStepInArgs skips consumed argv values and returns false for non-test invocations; zig build lint -Dtest-suites=does-not-exist exited 0; zig build test -Dtest-suites=does-not-exist failed with Unknown test suite id; zig build -p test source-lower -- --bogus exited 0
fingerprint.application: Reuse step-gated validation for future build options that should not abort lint or other non-target steps; keep regression tests for both false-positive step detection and scoped option validation.

fingerprint.learning: must stay synchronized with Zig's value-taking CLI options so can skip option payloads before matching step names, and the current regression tests should keep covering both consumed-value cases (, ) and an explicit token after options.
evidence: Verified in build.zig that the step scanner starts at argv[6], skips value-taking flags, stops at , and then matches .
passed with the current helper tests.
fingerprint.application: Use this as the proof lens when expanding the consumed-option list or adding new build CLI options.

fingerprint.learning: When build.zig needs to discover new artifact inputs, prefer one normalized scanner over adding another fallback parser, because duplicated walkers and resolver chains make the build surface harder to reason about and maintain.
evidence: build.zig: buildInvocationRequestsStepInArgs, parseTestFiltersAlloc, collectBuildInputTokensAlloc, collectBuildZigReferencedRepoZigPaths, collectImportedEmbedFileFunctionPatternsAlloc, collectEmbedFileBuildInputsForSource, artifactBuildInputPathsAlloc
fingerprint.application: Route new build-input coverage through a single model before adding more branchy fallback logic.

fingerprint.learning: When build.zig accepts post-`--` args for `zig build test`, normalize supported Zig test-runner split flags like `--seed 123` and `--cache-dir path` into equals form before forwarding; Zig 0.15.2 test binaries only accept `--seed=<n>`, `--cache-dir=<path>`, and `--listen=-`.
evidence: zig test build.zig --test-filter 'test runner args'
zig build test -Dtest-suites=root -- --seed 123
sed -n '41,53p' /opt/homebrew/Cellar/zig/0.15.2_1/lib/zig/compiler/test_runner.zig
fingerprint.application: Keep post-`--` parsing fail-closed for `--test-filter`, but treat supported runner flags as passthrough normalized to the syntax the Zig test runner actually consumes.

fingerprint.learning: When an outdated shift PR review comment names a concrete argv hazard, add that exact command shape as a build.zig regression test before resolving the thread.
evidence: build.zig:3006 covers '--prefix test lint -- --max-warnings 0' and keeps 'test' from being misdetected as a requested step.
zig test build.zig --test-filter 'step detection' passed after adding the regression.
zig build --summary none --prefix test lint -- --max-warnings 0 completed with 'No issues!' instead of test-arg rejection.
fingerprint.application: For stale review threads, freeze the original repro and turn it into a focused regression test even if the underlying logic already looks fixed.

fingerprint.learning: When build.zig normalizes post-'--' zig test runner split flags, treat omitted or flag-shaped values for --test-filter, --seed, and --cache-dir as invalid_user_input instead of silently consuming or dropping them.
evidence: zig test build.zig --test-filter 'test runner args'
zig build test -Dtest-suites=root -- --test-filter --seed=123
zig build test -Dtest-suites=root -- --seed --test-filter foo
fingerprint.application: In parseTestRunnerArgsAlloc, reject malformed split-form runner args before normalization and add exact regression tests for the malformed argv shapes named in review.

fingerprint.learning: When build.zig accepts post-'--' test controls, keep fail-closed parsing scoped to pure test invocations, because mixed-step commands like 'zig build lint test -- --max-warnings 0' must still route shared '--' args without test-only rejection.
evidence: zig build lint test -Dtest-suites=root -- --max-warnings 0
zig build test -Dtest-suites=root -- --test-filtre=foo
zig test build.zig
fingerprint.application: Gate requireTestRunnerArgs on buildInvocationRequestsOnlyStep('test'), reject unsupported pure-test post-'--' args explicitly, and keep regression tests for both mixed-step and pure-test command shapes.

fingerprint.learning: When build.zig infers requested steps from Zig build argv, mirror build_runner.zig's real token consumption rules: bare --system does not consume the next arg, but both --libc-runtimes and --glibc-runtimes do.
evidence: build.zig now removes --system from buildInvocationArgRequiresNextValue and adds --glibc-runtimes.
zig test build.zig passed with regressions for '--system test -- --seed=123' in both step-detection helpers and '--glibc-runtimes runtimes-dir test -- --seed=123' in exclusive test detection.
Zig 0.15.2 build_runner.zig handles --system as a bare flag and --libc-runtimes/--glibc-runtimes via nextArgOrFatal.
fingerprint.application: When review feedback cites argv drift from Zig's build runner, patch the local option table to the upstream contract and add exact command-shape regressions for every affected helper that shares the table.

fingerprint.learning: When public_root_package_contract_test mirrors the published package, keep repo-owned fixture trees free of temp-rooted symlinks or preserve symlink metadata instead of dereferencing external targets, because temp-rooted links make copyRepoFileIntoFixture fail with FileNotFound.
evidence: zig build test fails in test/public_root_package_contract_test.zig:161 while mirroring published_package_paths
find bench examples src test tools -type l -print -exec readlink {} \; shows test/compile_fail_inputs/.compile_fail_escape_helper_link.zig -> /var/folders/.../compile_fail_escape_helper_target.zig
fingerprint.application: Audit published-package fixture inputs for external temp symlinks before relying on public_root_package_contract_test as a repo-wide closure gate.

fingerprint.learning: When build.zig filters post-'--' zig test runner args, keep the accepted passthrough set aligned with Zig 0.15.2's compiler/test_runner contract, including exact support for --listen=- alongside --seed and --cache-dir.
evidence: Verified in /opt/homebrew/Cellar/zig/0.15.2_1/lib/zig/compiler/test_runner.zig and fixed in build.zig parser/tests.

Proof bundle passed: zig test build.zig --test-filter 'test runner args'; zig test build.zig --test-filter 'build invocation'; zig fmt --check; zig build lint -- --max-warnings 0; zig build; zig build test.
fingerprint.application: When review comments name a supported Zig test-runner flag that shift rejects, add the exact accepted form to testRunnerArgCanPassThrough, update the supported-forms error text, and cover it with a parser regression test before closure.

fingerprint.learning: When build.zig infers requested steps from Zig build argv, mirror compiler/build_runner.zig's visible argv contract: --debug-log still consumes the next token in Zig 0.15.2, while --system reaches the generated build helper as a bare flag because the parent process strips its pkgdir argument.
evidence: Zig 0.15.2 compiler/build_runner.zig parses --debug-log with nextArgOrFatal and documents --system as parent-consumed before the build runner sees argv.
Validated locally with zig test build.zig --test-filter 'build invocation', zig build lint -- --max-warnings 0, zig build, and zig build test --summary none.
fingerprint.application: Keep step-detection tables and regression tests aligned to build-runner-visible argv semantics, and add the exact review command shape as a unit test before treating a CLI-parsing review claim as actionable.

fingerprint.learning: When build.zig accepts zig build test passthrough args, validate --seed in both split and attached forms against Zig's unsigned 32-bit parser because malformed seeds otherwise bypass build-time checks and crash generated test runners.
evidence: build.zig:362-479 now validates --seed values before passthrough
zig test build.zig --test-filter 'test runner args' passed after adding split+attached invalid-seed coverage
zig build test -- --seed abc and zig build test -- --seed=abc now fail early with invalid user input instead of runner panic
fingerprint.application: Keep post-'--' passthrough parsing aligned with Zig test_runner.zig semantics and add regression tests for both split and attached flag spellings when new passthrough flags are allowed.

fingerprint.learning: When a build-only tool step in build.zig is meant to compile an internal executable, keep the step dependent on the executable build only and avoid addInstallArtifact because custom prefixes like -p test otherwise create stray bin trees as a side effect.
evidence: build.zig source-lower step no longer depends on addInstallArtifact for shift-source-lower
zig build source-lower passed
zig build -p "/prefix" source-lower proof returned NO_INSTALL_ARTIFACT
fingerprint.application: For internal tooling steps that users invoke for proof or inspection, separate build and install semantics so prefix overrides do not materialize unexpected output directories.

fingerprint.learning: When build.zig validates post-'--' test runner args, gate that parsing on whether the invocation requests the test step at all, not on test being the only requested step, or mixed-step commands silently skip invalid runner-arg validation.
evidence: build.zig:3420 wires requireTestRunnerArgs to test_requested instead of the pure-test-only predicate
zig test build.zig --test-filter 'build invocation'
zig build test source-lower -Dtest-suites=root -- --seed=abc -> Expected '--seed' to contain an unsigned 32-bit integer
fingerprint.application: For build.zig CLI routing changes, audit mixed-step step detection separately from pure-step-only behavior and add regression coverage for both predicates.

fingerprint.learning: When build.zig validates post-'--' test runner args from shared b.args, gate that parsing on exclusive test invocations, because mixed-step commands share one tail and non-test steps like lint may own flags such as --max-warnings.
evidence: build.zig now uses buildInvocationRequestsOnlyStep("test") before requireTestRunnerArgs.
zig test build.zig --test-filter 'build invocation' passed, including the mixed-step exclusive-detection regression for 'lint test -- --max-warnings 0'.

Direct repro 'zig build lint test -- --max-warnings 0' exited successfully after the fix, while full closure also passed: zig fmt --check build.zig, zig build lint -- --max-warnings 0, zig build, zig build test.
fingerprint.application: For future build.zig CLI review comments, treat shared post-'--' tails as ambiguous unless test is the only requested step, and prove the decision with both helper tests and the real mixed-step command shape.

fingerprint.learning: When build.zig validates test-specific options, gate both -Dtest-suites and post-'--' parsing on whether the invocation will actually execute the test step; mixed-step invocations with test should stay fail-closed, but help/list/fetch modes should stay fail-open.
evidence: zig test build.zig
zig build --summary none test source-lower -Dtest-suites=root -- --test-filtre=foo
zig build -h test -- --test-filtre=foo
fingerprint.application: Use a runnable-test-step predicate for build argv routing instead of exclusive-test detection, and treat non-executing discovery/exit modes as outside test validation.

fingerprint.learning: When a Zig build tool needs to install from both `zig build <step> --prefix ...` and `zig build install --prefix ...`, prefer wiring a single `addInstallArtifact` step into both the named step and `b.getInstallStep()`, because otherwise the named step can pass while the global install graph silently omits the binary.
evidence: build.zig now wires source_lowering_tool_install into both source_lowering_tool_step and b.getInstallStep().
`zig build source-lower --prefix <tmp>` produced bin/shift-source-lower after the fix.
`zig build install --prefix <tmp>` produced bin/shift-source-lower after wiring the global install step.
fingerprint.application: Use this pattern for installable internal tools and prove both install surfaces before closing build-step regressions.

fingerprint.learning: When build.zig validates post-'--' test-runner args, gate that parsing on pure test invocations so mixed-step commands like 'zig build lint test -- --max-warnings 0' do not lose the shared tail to test-only validation.
evidence: build.zig now calls requireTestRunnerArgs with buildInvocationRequestsOnlyStep('test') while preserving runnable-step detection separately.

Proof: zig test build.zig --test-filter 'mixed-step test keeps runnable detection without claiming the shared tail'; zig build lint test -- --max-warnings 0; zig build lint -- --max-warnings 0; zig build; zig build test.
fingerprint.application: Use exclusive-step detection for test-owned CLI tails, and add the exact mixed-step command shape as a build.zig regression test whenever a review comment names it.

fingerprint.learning: When build.zig mixes lint and test after --, prefer mixed shared-tail parsing that applies recognized test-runner args and ignores unknown foreign args because otherwise mixed invocations either drop test controls or reject valid lint flags.
evidence: zig test build.zig --test-filter 'test runner args'
zig build test source-lower -Dtest-suites=root -- --seed=abc
zig build lint test -- --max-warnings 0
fingerprint.application: Use mixed shared-tail parsing only for known foreign tail consumers like lint; keep strict parsing for pure test and test plus non-tail-consuming steps.

fingerprint.learning: When build.zig handles mixed 'lint test -- ...' invocations, prefer filtering recognized test-runner args out of lint's shared tail before constructing zlinter, because otherwise test-only flags like '--test-filter=foo' reach lint and fail with a zlinter usage error.
evidence: zig build lint test -- --max-warnings 0 --test-filter=foo failed with zlinter usage error before the fix and passed after wiring lint_shared_tail_args in build.zig.
zig test build.zig added focused coverage for stripping recognized test args and preserving unknown args in mixed shared tails.
fingerprint.application: Apply the split at the step fanout boundary and preserve unknown args for lint so mixed-step validation stays fail-closed.

gh pr checks 17 => no checks reported on the feature branch

fingerprint.application: For future shift $fin closeouts, verify divergence first, prove green locally when GitHub has no checks, then merge and confirm main is synced with the feature branch removed locally and remotely.

fingerprint.learning: When unifying shift.with onto effect_ir and ProgramPlan, treat the current lexical source/body lowerer as a structural gate, not parser debt: the shipped lexical contract includes optional/generated continuations, nested shift.with, non-eff effect parameter names, and witness flows outside the retained lowering subset.
evidence: src/internal/helper_body_lowering.zig only lowers the retained direct-op/helper subset; optional/generated continuation forms and nested shift.with do not fit that grammar.
test/lexical_with_test.zig and test/lexical_witness_test.zig exercise those unsupported lexical behaviors as part of the shipped contract.
fingerprint.application: Do not promise full lexical unification through the current source lowerer; route the work as a deeper compiler rewrite or an explicit scope decision first.

fingerprint.learning: When threading shift.with caller provenance into a compiler seam, keep the exact SourceLocation at the descriptor-run context type boundary because runtime SourceLocation fields stop comptime propagation and can break lexical preview dummy-value generation.
evidence: zig build test -Dtest-suites=lexical-with --summary none passed after restoring descriptorRunContext caller threading through src/with_api.zig and lexical descriptor call sites.
zig build --summary none passed with the same caller-provenance repair and non-failing lowering scaffolds in src/public_lowering.zig and src/internal/helper_body_lowering.zig.
zig build lint -- --max-warnings 0 passed after documenting the two intentional max_positional_args exceptions in src/with_api.zig and src/effect/generated_family.zig.
fingerprint.application: Thread caller provenance as a comptime parameter or type-level declaration until the compiler entrypoint consumes it, to avoid losing exact shift.with callsite information before sealed_engine or compiled-body dispatch can use it.

fingerprint.learning: When a named lexical body uses an error-union return signature such as anyerror!T, prefer parsing the return shape after the bang token before body lowering because the pre-bang tokens otherwise make the compiler treat the body as unit-returning and later trigger invalid ProgramPlan local references.
evidence: src/internal/source_graph_engine.zig parseReturnShape now scans for a bang token instead of only accepting a leading !.
zig build --summary none passed after the parseReturnShape repair.
zig build test -Dtest-suites=lexical-with --summary none passed after the same repair.
zig build lint -- --max-warnings 0 passed after the same repair.
fingerprint.application: When widening named-body lowering or other lexical compiler seams, normalize error-union return signatures before deriving value codecs and helper-body locals so later VM validation failures do not mask the real signature-classification bug.

fingerprint.learning: When NamedBody migration widens shipped lexical examples but nested-prompt witnesses still exceed the retained public lowering subset, fix the contract boundary instead of widening the lowerer blindly: keep shipped/example bodies source-canonical, quarantine static_redelim and multi_prompt as test-only runtime witnesses, and preserve helper ABI classification by treating typed underscore parameters as real value params unless their type is anytype.
evidence: zig build test --summary none passed only after removing witness.static_redelim and witness.multi_prompt from source_lowering canonical rows, switching src/witness_sources.zig nested-prompt cases to test-only anonymous bodies, and fixing src/internal/source_graph_engine.zig finalizeFunctionParam so classify(label, _: i32, _: anytype) keeps the i32 helper ABI.
fingerprint.application: For future lexical compiler seams in shift, separate public source-lowering guarantees from runtime semantic witnesses before widening helper/body lowering, and audit typed underscore parameters whenever helper ABI counts drift.

fingerprint.learning: When a shift fixed-point closure branch looks green on build and full tests after NamedBody remediation, rerun the full lint and public-boundary review before calling it ready, and reopen remediation immediately if the canonical shipped API still routes through a repo-owned lowering helper instead of the caller-owned source witness path.
evidence: This turn fixed the live PR findings in src/internal/program_plan.zig and src/internal/program_plan_interpreter.zig and kept zig build test --summary none green, but zig build lint -- --max-warnings 0 still failed on new lexical seam files and review of src/with_api.zig plus src/public_lowering.zig showed shift.NamedBody(...) still compiles through lowerAt(...) rather than the caller-owned lower(...) path.
fingerprint.application: Use fixed-point-driver in shift to treat lint closure and downstream/public-boundary viability as separate final gates after tests, especially when a new canonical API is introduced.

fingerprint.learning: When a shift fixed-point closure introduces a new canonical API path that cannot yet satisfy the broader public contract, prefer an explicit fail-closed boundary in code, docs, and package-contract tests before calling the branch ready, because otherwise build/test green can mask a materially false shipped claim.
evidence: This turn fixed the compile-time log and underscored after-hook regression, closed seam-file lint, then bounded shift.NamedBody() to the admitted package-owned compiled subset in README and public_root_package_contract_test while the full closure bundle passed: zig build --summary none, zig build test --summary none, zig build lint -- --max-warnings 0, zig fmt --check build.zig src examples test bench source_graph_embed.zig.
fingerprint.application: During future fixed-point-driver closure in shift, treat public-boundary truth as a separate final gate and prefer an explicit bounded claim over an overstated API promise when the structural extension is not yet proven.

fingerprint.learning: When trying to compile caller-owned anonymous lexical bodies through shift.with, do not assume wrapper-level @src() or synthetic source reconstruction will stay comptime-stable in Zig; if caller provenance must survive the root package boundary, prefer an explicit caller-owned source witness surface because otherwise the implementation can stall on callee-owned @src() and non-comptime body-expression slices.
evidence: This turn the source-ownership probe showed wrapper calls capture callee-owned @src(), not caller-owned @src(); then a downstream root-package probe with withCallerSource/withCallerSourceAndContent still failed because the anonymous-body synthetic source path in src/with_api.zig could not turn the extracted body expression into a fully comptime-known string for public_lowering.sourceWithContent(...).
fingerprint.application: For the reopened broader plan in shift, treat explicit caller participation in the public source-identity surface as the leading candidate before spending more time on automatic anonymous-body recovery through wrappers.

fingerprint.learning: When a caller-owned root-package lexical witness appends body_source bytes, inspect the exact generated root source first and require raw 'pub fn ...' text, because double-escaped multiline-string backslashes make the source graph miss the requested entry symbol and misreport the failure as EntryMissing.
evidence: A focused source-graph probe on witnessSyntheticSource found __owned_body as soon as the appended bytes started with raw top-level function text.

The downstream package-contract fixture emitted four leading backslashes in generated body_source lines; fixing those lines advanced the failure from public_lowering EntryMissing to helper_body_lowering subset rejection.
fingerprint.application: For explicit caller-owned source witnesses, debug source visibility before changing lowering logic, then treat any remaining failure as a later-stage body-lowering limitation.

fingerprint.learning: When a durable plan is meant to govern architecture rather than a tactical patch slice, prefer restating the current plan in the architecture's public API -> lowering -> runtime-contract pipeline terms, because a completed sub-slice can otherwise be mistaken for completion of the real goal.
evidence: This turn corrected the shift durable plan from an explicit caller-owned witness slice to the governing pipeline: generalized algebraic effects public API -> effect_ir internal lowering -> ProgramPlan execution contract.
The repaired durable steps are now st-1200 through st-1204, and update_plan was resynced to the same execution spine.
fingerprint.application: Use pipeline-shaped durable steps for shift architecture work so later fixed-point loops stay anchored to the real public-to-runtime goal instead of the last tactical seam.

fingerprint.learning: When a compiled lexical path in shift already derives binding schemas before execution, prefer enriching ProgramPlan with lifecycle/output metadata and consuming those tags in the interpreter, because otherwise ProgramPlan remains a lossy control graph instead of the real execution contract.
evidence: This turn routed compiled lexical paths in src/with_api.zig through enriched ProgramPlan values built from lexical_bundle_schema.BindingSchemas(...) and used those plans for execution.

src/internal/program_plan_interpreter.zig now finalizes outputs from RequirementPlan.output_tag metadata instead of only probing handlers heuristically, and focused proof stayed green under program-plan review, program bridge, and public-root-package-contract lanes.

fingerprint.learning: When a public caller-owned lexical API needs to distinguish callsite routing from supplied source bytes, prefer an explicit route enum instead of inferring compilation from a nullable source override, because nullability can silently bypass the advertised caller-owned path; likewise, when a synthesized authored ProgramPlan has resume_type void, emit return_unit-local sizing and terminators to avoid invalid return_value shapes.

fingerprint.application: For future full-suite performance work in shift, start by deleting duplicated proof lanes and whole-root imports before touching runtime logic, then use process cleanup plus parallel lexical slicing only after the expensive semantic scans are bounded.

fingerprint.learning: When a shift fixed-point proof-surface change reopens the long lexical-with lane without changing shipped behavior, prefer retracting the new probe surface and keeping only the minimal contract/test correction, because the narrow tree can restore healthy downstream package-contract progress while preserving the real review fix.
evidence: After retracting the added cross-module source-ownership probe harness in build.zig and test/source_ownership_probe_{helper,test}.zig, zig build test -Dtest-suites=source-ownership-probe still passed and zig build test -Dtest-suites=lexical-with -- --test-filter=generated family handleWithErrorSetAt preserves caller provenance still passed.
A fresh repo-wide closure wave then passed: zig fmt --check on all Zig files, zig build lint -- --max-warnings 0, zig build, zig build bench-state-effect, and zig build test.
fingerprint.application: For future shift review-remediation under full proof, reject proof-surface expansion that only restates wrapper ownership semantics if it destabilizes the slow suite; keep the shipped fix narrow and prove it with the smallest honest targeted lane plus the final full closure wave.

fingerprint.learning: When program_frontend still emits public witness IDs in shift, keep source_lowering witness admission rows and source_lowering_witness_completion_test coverage in lockstep so deleted canonical cases cannot silently regress to UnsupportedSourceCase.
evidence: src/program_frontend.zig still emits witness.multi_prompt and witness.static_redelim while src/source_lowering.zig had dropped both witness rows from promotedSupportedCase(.witness).

Restoring the two canonical witness cases plus extending test/source_lowering_witness_completion_test.zig closed the regression, and zig fmt --check, zig build lint -- --max-warnings 0, zig build, and zig build test --summary none all passed.
fingerprint.application: When review comments mention removed source-lowering witness support, first diff program_frontend emitted IDs against source_lowering promoted witness admissions, then add or extend witness completion coverage before full closure.

fingerprint.learning: When shift relies on checked-in repo_zig_paths.txt for gitless/package builds, keep it in parity with tracked *.zig files via a build.zig test so missing new suites fail closed instead of breaking archived lexical/source-lowering builds later.
evidence: build.zig parity test compares repoZigPathRegistryAlloc(...) against repo_zig_paths.txt after the lexical_with_named_body and source_lowering_promoted files were added.
Clean temp-checkout zig build test -Dtest-suites=lexical-with passed after refreshing repo_zig_paths.txt.
fingerprint.application: Refresh repo_zig_paths.txt alongside Zig test/layout changes and preserve the parity test as the guardrail for gitless package consumers.

fingerprint.learning: When restoring the source-compatible shift.with(runtime, handlers, Body) entrypoint, prefer adding a public withAt(caller, runtime, handlers, Body) seam and updating every mirrored lexical surface plus repo-owned explicit @src() call site in the same patch, because examples, internal shims, and contract fixtures otherwise fail to compile against the restored wrapper.
evidence: review comment cited src/with_api.zig public entrypoint breakage
zig build lint -- --max-warnings 0
zig build
zig build test
fingerprint.application: Use with(...) as the compatibility wrapper, route explicit provenance through withAt(...), and migrate internal lexical mirrors plus examples/tests before closing the review loop.

fingerprint.learning: When fixing retained Program runtime forwarders, route caller-aware wrappers through withAt and add a root-wired regression that instantiates compat.run, Program.run, and Program.runAt, because module-local compat tests may be outside the default zig build test surface.
evidence: src/internal/program_runtime.zig and src/internal/lexical_runtime.zig used with_api.with(caller, runtime, handlers, Body) even though with_api.with only accepts runtime, handlers, Body.

zig build test -- --test-filter 'retained compat program entrypoints stay wired through caller-aware forwarding' passed after switching the forwarders to with_api.withAt and adding the root test in src/root.zig.

Full zig build test did not produce a verdict after more than six minutes; the active compiler process stayed on test/lexical_with_named_body_test.zig, so commit/push must stay blocked until that suite completes or is scoped.
fingerprint.application: For review-remediation closure on retained front doors, keep the seam fix minimal, land proof in a default-wired test module, and report full-suite non-verdicts explicitly instead of claiming green closure.

fingerprint.learning: When trying to make shift's zig build test more reliable by sharding a hot suite, sequencing the run steps is not enough: the next compile artifacts can still start in parallel. Put the dependency on the downstream addTest compile steps themselves, using the previous run step as the gate, so the suite advances one artifact at a time.
evidence: build.zig lexical-with sequencing via run-step dependOn still showed four concurrent zig test compilers for lexical_with_test, lexical_with_named_body_test, lexical_with_named_body_optional_test, and lexical_with_named_body_generated_test.
After moving the dependency edges onto lexical_with_named_body*_tests.step, ps showed a single active lexical-with zig test compiler instead of parallel fanout.
fingerprint.application: For build-graph reliability work in shift, verify concurrency fixes with a process-tree check, and if the goal is bounded verdicts rather than peak throughput, gate compile steps rather than only aggregate run steps.

fingerprint.learning: When sharding shift's lexical-with suite into new repo-owned Zig files, prefer merging repo_zig_paths.txt with git-tracked paths during local registry construction, because otherwise public-lowering ownership checks reject the new files until they are staged.
evidence: A clean zig build test -Dtest-suites=lexical-with --summary none failed in test/lexical_with_fixture_test.zig with 'public lowering source path must resolve to an owned repo file' even though repo_zig_paths.txt already listed the file.
Updating build.zig collectRepoZigPathsAlloc to union tracked paths and repo_zig_paths.txt removed that ownership failure on the next clean lexical-with run.
fingerprint.application: For local shift proof waves that add new test files, keep filesystem discovery as the last fallback but let the checked-in registry extend tracked paths so source ownership checks see new repo-owned files before commit time.

fingerprint.learning: When a serialized shift lexical-with lane still has one transcript-heavy fixture hotspot, split transcript parity from generated/runtime behavior so the next active worker becomes a smaller, more bounded shard rather than the whole bundle, because otherwise one mixed fixture file still dominates the lane.
evidence: test/lexical_with_fixture_test.zig was 442 lines and was the next clean lexical-with hotspot after the initial sharding and sequencing fixes.

After splitting it into test/lexical_with_fixture_test.zig (99 lines) and test/lexical_with_fixture_control_test.zig (195 lines), the next clean focused run advanced to the smaller control-fixture shard instead of the original 442-line bundle.
fingerprint.application: For shift test-reliability remediation, keep the serialized build graph and keep splitting the currently active hotspot by semantic cluster until the clean lexical-with lane stops being dominated by one file.

fingerprint.learning: When a serialized shift lexical-with hotspot stays dominant even after aggressive line-count splits, prefer reasoning about compile-weight seams like NamedBody and public-lowering paths instead of assuming smaller files alone will restore fast verdicts, because one tiny shard can still dominate the lane.
evidence: The former 195-line control-fixture shard was split into a 63-line control shard, an 87-line optional shard, and a 71-line resource shard.

A clean focused zig build test -Dtest-suites=lexical-with --summary none still advanced to the 63-line control-fixture shard as the active worker, indicating the remaining cost is not explained by line count alone.
fingerprint.application: For the next shift reliability loop, investigate the semantic compile cost of the active shard (for example NamedBody/public-lowering/transcript machinery) before continuing to cut by file size.

fingerprint.learning: When a shift lexical-with hotspot comes from non-essential NamedBody usage in ordinary fixture/runtime tests, remove NamedBody from those tests first, because the clean serialized lane should spend its compile budget in the dedicated NamedBody shard rather than in unrelated control/runtime fixtures.
evidence: After removing NamedBody from test/lexical_with_fixture_control_test.zig and test/lexical_with_runtime_test.zig, a clean zig build test -Dtest-suites=lexical-with --summary none advanced to test/lexical_with_named_body_test.zig instead of the fixture/runtime shards.
fingerprint.application: For the next reliability loop, treat time in lexical_with_named_body_* as intentional compile weight and focus any further reductions there rather than continuing to optimize non-NamedBody fixture tests.

fingerprint.learning: When a shift lexical-with NamedBody shard still dominates after being reduced to one or two tests, stop splitting by file and treat repo-owned NamedBody lowering itself as the expensive seam, because additional file cuts just add orchestration noise without reducing the underlying compile cost.
evidence: A clean serialized zig build test -Dtest-suites=lexical-with --summary none kept test/lexical_with_named_body_test.zig active for nearly a minute even after the file was reduced to a single real state-handler test.
Earlier cuts moved cost out of non-NamedBody fixture/runtime shards, but the dedicated NamedBody shard still dominated once those unrelated seams were removed.
fingerprint.application: For the next shift reliability pass, investigate NamedBody/public-lowering compile mechanics or change the proof surface, rather than continuing to split NamedBody tests into smaller files.

fingerprint.learning: When a shift lexical-with NamedBody hotspot still dominates even after splitting test files and support files by family, treat repo-owned NamedBody lowering itself as the remaining expensive seam, because narrower source paths alone did not collapse the compile time.
evidence: A clean serialized zig build test -Dtest-suites=lexical-with --summary none still kept test/lexical_with_named_body_test.zig active for more than forty seconds after its support was narrowed to test/lexical_with_named_body_basic_support.zig.
The support split succeeded mechanically and preserved ownership, but the active worker did not shift to optional or generated NamedBody shards within the observation window.
fingerprint.application: For the next shift reliability pass, inspect the compile-time path behind repo-owned NamedBody/public-lowering itself or change the proof surface, rather than continuing to split NamedBody sources by family.

fingerprint.learning: When a clean serialized shift lexical-with lane still spends nearly a minute in the basic repo-owned NamedBody shard after deduplicating maybeLowerAt and splitting support files by family, investigate NamedBody/public-lowering semantics or move some proof burden off the default lexical-with lane because further test-graph sharding no longer reduces the dominant compile cost.
evidence: with_api now computes namedCompiledLexicalPlan once and reuses it for both fail-closed checking and compiled execution instead of calling public_lowering.maybeLowerAt twice for the same repo-owned NamedBody body.

A clean zig build test -Dtest-suites=lexical-with --summary none still kept test/lexical_with_named_body_test.zig active for roughly forty-five seconds after that refactor and the family-specific support split.
fingerprint.application: For the next shift reliability pass, stop optimizing the lexical-with test graph first and inspect compile-time work inside repo-owned NamedBody lowering itself or redesign the proof surface around that seam.

fingerprint.learning: When shift's default lexical-with gate is made reliable by moving heavy repo-owned NamedBody conformance into an explicit suite, add a default_enabled flag to build.zig test suite specs and keep one separate lexical-with-namedbody lane, because the clean core lane can then finish while the heavy conformance lane remains available on demand.
evidence: zig test build.zig --test-filter 'test suite selection' passed after adding TestSuiteSpec.default_enabled and a regression that disables one suite by default.

zig build test -Dtest-suites=lexical-with --summary none returned cleanly, while zig build test -Dtest-suites=lexical-with-namedbody --summary none still exercised the heavy NamedBody shard explicitly.
fingerprint.application: For shift proof-surface remediation, keep the default zig build test focused on the lightest honest lexical coverage and expose heavier repo-owned NamedBody conformance as an explicit suite rather than forcing both through the same default gate.

fingerprint.learning: When shift's heavy repo-owned NamedBody conformance is split into an explicit lexical-with-namedbody suite and the core lexical-with lane is left as the default-enabled suite, keep both proofs: the core lane should return cleanly, and the opt-in lane should still reproduce the expensive NamedBody shard because that proves the separation is real rather than accidental.
evidence: zig test build.zig --test-filter 'test suite selection' passed after adding TestSuiteSpec.default_enabled and the default-enabled regression.

zig build test -Dtest-suites=lexical-with --summary none returned cleanly while zig build test -Dtest-suites=lexical-with-namedbody --summary none still exercised test/lexical_with_named_body_test.zig as the active heavy shard.
fingerprint.application: For future shift proof-surface work, treat the default lexical gate and the explicit NamedBody conformance lane as separate contracts and optimize them independently instead of trying to force both into one default path.

fingerprint.learning: When shift's heavy repo-owned NamedBody conformance lane is transformed into a single registry-driven runner, keep the default lexical-with gate separate and verify that the opt-in lane now compiles one namedbody_conformance_runner module instead of the old stack of per-shard NamedBody test modules, because that proves the proof surface changed even before the heavy lane is made fast.
evidence: zig build test -Dtest-suites=lexical-with --summary none returned cleanly after the suite split into lexical-with-core and lexical-with-namedbody.

A clean zig build test -Dtest-suites=lexical-with-namedbody --summary none showed one active namedbody_conformance_runner.zig compile worker instead of the prior lexical_with_named_body_* module stack.
fingerprint.application: For the next shift conformance pass, optimize the registry-driven runner itself or its case mix, not the old per-file NamedBody shard graph, because the heavy lane is now transformed into the intended single-module proof surface.

fingerprint.learning: When transforming shift's heavy repo-owned NamedBody conformance into an explicit registry-driven runner, keep the default lexical-with gate separate and verify that the heavy lane now compiles one namedbody_conformance_runner module because that proves the proof surface changed even if the transformed lane is still expensive.
evidence: zig build test -Dtest-suites=lexical-with --summary none returned cleanly after the lexical-with-core split remained the default-enabled suite.
zig build test -Dtest-suites=lexical-with-namedbody --summary none now compiled one test/namedbody_conformance_runner.zig worker instead of the old lexical_with_named_body_* shard stack.
fingerprint.application: For future shift work, optimize the registry-driven namedbody runner or its case mix directly instead of returning to per-file NamedBody shard surgery, because the proof surface is now the intended single-module explicit lane.

fingerprint.learning: When a transformed shift NamedBody conformance lane is still heavy, split the contract into a smoke lexical-with-namedbody suite and a separate full namedbody-conformance-full suite, because the default explicit lane needs bounded proof while the exhaustive registry can remain available as a deeper audit path.
evidence: zig build test -Dtest-suites=lexical-with --summary none returned cleanly after lexical-with-core remained the default gate.

The transformed lexical-with-namedbody lane compiled one namedbody_conformance_runner.zig worker; it remained expensive enough that a further smoke-vs-full contract split is the next honest proof-surface refinement.

fingerprint.learning: When shift's repo-owned NamedBody conformance is transformed into separate smoke and full registry-driven runners, verify that the default lexical-with gate exits cleanly and that the two explicit heavy suites compile one runner module each, because that proves the proof surface changed from per-shard source tests into bounded explicit contracts.

A clean process snapshot during the focused runs showed one namedbody_conformance_runner.zig worker for lexical-with-namedbody and one namedbody_conformance_full_runner.zig worker for namedbody-conformance-full, while the default lexical-with lane had already exited.

fingerprint.learning: When a clean shift zig build test run still sits in the top-level build wrapper with no visible active inner zig test worker after the default heavy suites were split into smoke and explicit full lanes, treat build-step orchestration itself as the next seam to diagnose because more suite carving is no longer producing a trustworthy top-level verdict.
evidence: After splitting lexical-with, NamedBody conformance, open-row-lowering, and source-lowering-completion into smoke versus explicit full suites, a fresh zig build test still remained in the .zig-cache build wrapper for over a minute with no visible active child zig test process.

fingerprint.learning: When a shifted default zig build test is blocked by huge suite matrices, keep carving those matrices into smoke versus explicit full suites and then re-run the top-level gate from a clean process tree, because reliable default verdicts return once the default-enabled proofs are reduced to bounded contracts.

fingerprint.learning: When shift snapshot-drift tests need to rewrite helper source files, move the mutation onto a dedicated tracked fixture because shared repo helpers can make full-suite SourceDrifted results flaky under parallel test execution.
