fingerprint.learning: When shift changes ArtifactV1 wire bytes, prefer bumping the artifact header version and decoding each supported version with explicit instruction/function-row maps, because reusing current enum ordinals or repurposed reserved bytes under the old version bit silently breaks cross-version artifact exchange.
evidence: The review surfaced two concrete compatibility breaks: renumbered serialized instruction tags and repurposed function-table reserved bytes for result_codec while the header still advertised ArtifactV1 version 1.

The fix in src/agent_vm_artifact.zig now emits artifact version 2, decodes both v1 and v2 headers, remaps legacy v1 instruction tags explicitly, and treats v1 function bytes 9..11 as reserved while v2 reads result_codec from those bytes.

Proof closed with zig fmt --check, zig build lint -- --max-warnings 0, zig build, zig build test, plus targeted ArtifactV1 compatibility tests for legacy v1 instruction tags and v1/v2 function-table bytes.
fingerprint.application: For future ArtifactV1 reviews in shift, treat any on-disk byte-layout change as a format-version change first and add explicit cross-version decode tests before shipping, to avoid silent decoder drift across branches or releases.

evidence: src/with_api.zig now uses exact equality in namedBodyModulePathMatchesExternalSourcePath and adds a regression for x.a.helpers vs a/helpers.zig.
fingerprint.application: For NamedBody provenance or withOwnedSource review comments, pressure-test both longer-prefix and shorter-suffix spoof paths and add a direct matcher regression before closure.

fingerprint.learning: When validating downstream external shift.NamedBody descriptors, require exact source_path equality against the body_fn provenance instead of suffix-only module-path matching, because shortened paths like a/helpers.zig otherwise forge source identity and bypass provenance checks.
evidence: src/with_api.zig:79-87 accepts dotted suffix matches in namedBodyModulePathMatchesCandidate.
src/with_api.zig:137-149 uses that matcher to admit NamedBody declarations.
A downstream temp consumer with @import("x/a/helpers.zig") and shift.NamedBody("a/helpers.zig", "body", anyerror!i32, &helpers.body) built successfully in this review turn.
fingerprint.application: Tighten external NamedBody provenance to exact path equality and add a smoke test that rejects shortened suffix paths, not only unrelated fake longer paths.

fingerprint.learning: When shift public-root package smoke mirrors deps/shift into a temp consumer, rewrite mandatory mirrored build.zig.zon dependencies like zlinter to local path deps from the cache and require exact external NamedBody source_path matches, or downstream package-contract closure can hang on network and miss suffix-spoof provenance bugs.
evidence: test/public_root_package_contract_smoke_test.zig now mirrors cached zlinter into deps/zlinter and rewrites deps/shift/build.zig.zon to .path = "../zlinter" before downstream builds.
src/with_api.zig now routes non-repo NamedBody source paths through exact namedBodyModulePathMatchesExternalSourcePath equality and adds an x.a.helpers vs a/helpers.zig regression.
fingerprint.application: For future fixed-point review closure on package-boundary or NamedBody provenance work, rerun zig build test -Dtest-suites=public-root-package-contract after making the mirrored fixture hermetic and add an explicit shortened-suffix spoof regression.

fingerprint.learning: When reviewing .const_i32 handling for .usize locals, treat ProgramPlan.validate and both runtimes as an intentional unsigned-bit reinterpretation path, and add a direct regression witness instead of relying on .const_usize hex coverage.
evidence: src/internal/program_plan.zig:347-374
src/internal/program_plan_interpreter.zig:447-454
src/artifact_vm_runtime.zig:436-451
fingerprint.application: Put the witness in test/program_plan_review_regression_test.zig so validation and both runtime decoders stay aligned.

fingerprint.learning: When shift explicit IR plans target a .usize local, keep .const_i32 invalid and require .const_usize; both ProgramPlan.validate and both execution engines must fail closed on malformed .const_i32 -> .usize plans instead of unsigned bit reinterpretation.
evidence: src/internal/program_plan.zig now rejects .const_i32 unless dst_codec == .i32 and adds an InvalidInstructionLocalIndex regression for .usize locals.
src/internal/program_plan_interpreter.zig and src/artifact_vm_runtime.zig now return error.ProgramContractViolation for malformed .const_i32 -> .usize plans and each has a dedicated regression test.
fingerprint.application: For future ProgramPlan literal or schema-compatibility work, preserve validator/runtime agreement by using .const_usize as the only unambiguous usize literal encoding and add direct malformed-plan rejection tests in validation and both runtimes.

fingerprint.learning: When evaluating whether shift can assume .git is always available, first verify whether published-package and mirrored-fixture workflows are still supported, because build.zig and the public-root package smoke suite intentionally run without checkout metadata.
evidence: build.zig falls back from tracked git discovery to repo_zig_paths.txt and then filesystem scanning when repoRootContainsGitMetadata is false.
test/public_root_package_contract_smoke_test.zig publishes and mirrors a package path set that includes repo_zig_paths.txt and source_graph_embed.zig rather than relying on a live .git directory.
fingerprint.application: For future cleanup or simplification work around repo_zig_paths.txt, decide explicitly whether non-git package consumers remain in scope before replacing the fallback ownership authority with a git-only source of truth.

fingerprint.learning: When shift wants git metadata to be authoritative and filesystem scanning to be the only backup for repo-owned Zig discovery, keep the repo_zig_paths registry interface but source it from git first and filesystem walk second, because that removes repo_zig_paths.txt without rewriting source_graph_embed, public_lowering, or with_api.
evidence: build.zig now drops collectRepoZigPathsFromRegistryFile and collectRepoZigPathsAlloc falls back directly from tracked git discovery to collectFilesystemRepoZigPaths.

build.zig.zon and test/public_root_package_contract_smoke_test.zig no longer publish repo_zig_paths.txt, and zig test build.zig --test-filter "repo Zig path registry" plus zig build test -Dtest-suites=public-root-package-contract --summary none both passed after deletion.
fingerprint.application: For future root cleanup or ownership-authority changes in shift, prefer replacing the source of an existing authority seam before widening consumer semantics or rewriting all call sites.

fingerprint.learning: When shift makes git-tracked Zig paths authoritative for repo-owned discovery, prefer deleting unreferenced tracked tmp_*.zig probes in the same cleanup wave, because otherwise they are admitted into lint, registry, and ownership surfaces as real repo inputs.
evidence: After repo-owned discovery was switched to git-first plus filesystem-backup, tmp_invalid_ir_test.zig, tmp_ir_entry_param_test.zig, and tmp_row_only_param_compile_test.zig were tracked, had no live references outside themselves, and were therefore included by the tracked-path collector.
Deleting those three files still left zig fmt --check on live files, zig build lint -- --max-warnings 0, zig build --summary none, and zig build test green.
fingerprint.application: Use the full repo closure gate to prove removal whenever tracked root tmp_*.zig files are no longer referenced.

fingerprint.learning: When a shift module relies on @embedFile for repo-root siblings such as examples/, tests, and build.zig, keep that embed surface at repo root or redesign the asset-loading seam, because moving the module under src/ makes those embeds resolve outside the package path.
evidence: Moving source_graph_embed.zig to src/internal caused zig build and zig build test to fail with "embed of file outside package path" for paths like ../../examples/open_row_choice_basic.zig and ../../examples/resource_basic.zig.

Restoring source_graph_embed.zig to the repo root while keeping the repo_zig_paths cleanup and tmp_*.zig deletions preserved green closure for zig fmt --check on live files, zig test build.zig --test-filter "repo Zig path registry", zig build test -Dtest-suites=public-root-package-contract --summary none, zig build lint -- --max-warnings 0, zig build --summary none, and zig build test.
fingerprint.application: For future source_graph_embed cleanup, treat relocation as an architectural change that needs a new embedding mechanism, not a path-only refactor.

fingerprint.learning: When starting the shift Zig 0.16.0 upgrade, prefer creating and switching to a dedicated feature branch from main before any migration edits because it keeps the upgrade scope isolated and reviewable.
evidence: git switch -c feature/zig-0.16.0-upgrade from main at 467ad669a97b3b50d4ee34d632007ca3f090b9d7
fingerprint.application: Start the Zig 0.16.0 migration work on feature/zig-0.16.0-upgrade instead of editing main directly.

fingerprint.learning: When migrating shift to stable Zig 0.16.0, prefer restoring the default zig build path first and fail-closing dev-only steps like lint or profiler helpers when their upstream dependencies require incompatible compiler APIs because that keeps the user-visible compile target moving without forcing a wider toolchain upgrade.
evidence: zig version => 0.16.0; zig build exited 0 after build.zig/source/example updates; zig build test --test-timeout 500ms still failed in separate test-only migration surfaces; lint/zprof steps were left explicit fail-closed because current zlinter/zprof pins are not stable-0.16.0 compatible
fingerprint.application: On the next stable Zig migration pass, fix repo-owned build and source APIs first, then reopen lint/profiler/test-lane follow-up work only after the default build is green.

fingerprint.learning: When a stable Zig upgrade must keep tool dependencies working but upstream lacks a stable-compatible transitive pin, prefer a small repo-local compat package plus explicit lint excludes for vendored and fetched dependency trees because that restores the tool step without letting third-party findings drown the repo-owned verdict.
evidence: tools/vendor/zlinter patched to zls 0.16.0 tag and version string; zig build lint -- --max-warnings 0 ran end-to-end and reported repo-owned findings after excluding tools/vendor and zig-pkg; build.zig.zon updated zprof to v4.0.0 and zig build zprof-hotspots exited 0
fingerprint.application: For future shift toolchain upgrades, keep the root build green first, then vendor only the smallest missing stable-compat tool surface and exclude third-party trees from lint so remaining failures represent repo work.

fingerprint.learning: When clearing shift Zig 0.16 no_deprecated warnings in owned tests, prefer replacing std.fs.path.* with std.Io.Dir.path.* and std.mem.indexOf* with std.mem.find* first, because those aliases now trip the lint gate without changing test semantics.
evidence: After those direct replacements in the five owned test files, 'zig build lint -- --max-warnings 0' reported no deprecated warnings for the owned paths.
fingerprint.application: For test-only deprecation cleanup, rg owned files for std.fs.path.* and std.mem.indexOf* before broader API churn so the fix stays minimal and behavior-preserving.

fingerprint.learning: When shift token-walking helpers only care about a small subset of std.zig.Token.Tag values, replace partial enum switches with explicit if-chains and add loop-target labels for nested continues; this clears zlinter's exhaustive-switch and require_labeled_continue findings without changing behavior.
evidence: src/internal/source_graph_engine.zig:182-225, 924-955, 1599-1751
src/internal/helper_body_lowering.zig:116-123, 180-241, 501-661
fingerprint.application: Prefer control-flow spelling changes over giant enum case lists when lint is enforcing exhaustive switches on tokenizer tags.

fingerprint.learning: When clearing a large Zig 0.16 lint backlog in shift, prefer narrowing the lint surface to repo-owned files first and then fixing findings by category because that prevents vendored dependency noise from hiding the governing cleanup seams.
evidence: build.zig excludes tools/vendor and zig-pkg from zlinter paths; command outcome: 'zig build lint -- --max-warnings 0' ended with 'No issues!'; repo-owned hot files included build.zig, src/agent_vm_artifact.zig, src/internal/helper_body_lowering.zig, and src/with_api.zig
fingerprint.application: On the next shift lint remediation wave, exclude third-party trees up front and clear one warning class at a time before rerunning the full lint step.

fingerprint.learning: When Zig 0.16 build.zig step detection changes, prefer recovering requested steps from host argv and updating the local value-consuming flag table together, because hard-coded step fallbacks and stale flag parsing can misroute non-test invocations through test-only validation.
evidence: Behavior proof: 'zig build test -- --bogus' now fails with an unsupported test-tail error instead of exiting 0.
Regression proof: 'zig build lint -Dtest-suites=bogus' now reaches lint cleanly, and 'zig build -h -Dtest-suites=bogus' prints help instead of dying in test-suite validation.
Closure proof: 'zig build lint -- --max-warnings 0', 'zig build', and 'zig build test' all passed after the repair.
fingerprint.application: When build.zig CLI migrations touch step detection, verify the exact command shapes first and keep helper tables aligned with compiler/build_runner.zig before trusting mixed-step behavior.

fingerprint.learning: When shift removes a vendored Zig dependency during a Zig 0.16 migration, restore the external pin and route wrapper modules through one shared owner module because otherwise repo-owned trust boundaries widen and benchmark builds can fail on duplicate module ownership.
evidence: build.zig.zon now pins zlinter to git+https://github.com/KurtWagner/zlinter?ref=0.16.x#9b4d67b9725e7137ac876cc628fe5dd2ca5a2681, tools/vendor/zlinter was deleted, and zig build lint -- --max-warnings 0 exited 0.

zig build bench-runtime-backends --summary none exited 0 after src/lexical_runtime_internal.zig re-exported through shift_shared and bench/runtime_backend_matrix_bench.zig moved to Zig 0.16 Io timing APIs.
fingerprint.application: On future Zig dependency cleanups in shift, de-vendor third-party trees first and fix module ownership at the shared wrapper seam before debugging benchmark-specific wiring.

fingerprint.learning: When fixing public shift compile regressions, prefer proving a downstream path dependency consumer first because repo-only tests can miss public comptime failures.
evidence: Downstream path dependency package reproduced a branch-quota failure in source_graph_embed.registryContainsLine for shift.NamedBody(@src().file, ...); after the fix, zig build test passed.
A separate downstream executable using shift.withOwnedSource with an anonymous body built successfully after the comptime-lowering repair.
fingerprint.application: Run an external path dependency repro alongside repo gates before closing future review claims on public lexical APIs.

fingerprint.learning: In shift build.zig shared-tail handling, treat --test-timeout as a build-only test signal: recognize it for unsupported-host test inference and lint arg stripping, but never forward it to spawned test binaries.
evidence: zig build test -- --test-timeout=10 --test-filter "shared-tail invocation inference recovers test timeout without argv inspection" failed when --test-timeout was normalized into test_runner_args.passthrough, then passed after the arg became build-only.
zig test build.zig passed after recognizedTestRunnerArgSpan and lintSharedTailArgsAlloc were updated to consume --test-timeout as a recognized test signal without passthrough.
fingerprint.application: When adding post-`--` test args in build.zig, separate build-only signals from child-test passthrough and keep unsupported-host mixed lint/test fallback fail-closed by stripping recognized test args from lint whenever test is inferred but lint step attribution is unknown.

fingerprint.learning: When shift $fin sees 0 0 divergence and no GitHub checks, prefer reporting nothing new to push, rerunning the local closeout gates, and merging with gh pr merge --squash --delete-branch --match-head-commit because this avoids false push claims and keeps merge receipts explicit.
evidence: PR #19 on feature/zig-0.16.0-upgrade had git rev-list --left-right --count origin/feature/zig-0.16.0-upgrade...HEAD = 0 0, gh pr checks 19 reported no checks, and gh pr view 19 showed mergeable.

Local gates passed: zig fmt --check on tracked Zig files, zig build lint -- --max-warnings 0, zig build, zig build test --test-timeout 500ms, zig build source-lower --prefix <tmp>, and zig build install --prefix <tmp>.

Guarded merge receipt: gh pr merge 19 --squash --delete-branch --match-head-commit f8ea001c60e320b17953859bea2b2d3438b00ad0; final PR merge commit 60abcca499db33c2e43c346944db23fc1c5be9e7; main...origin/main = 0 0.
fingerprint.application: Use this closeout sequence for future shift $fin turns when GitHub reports no branch checks, then verify final main parity before reporting completion.

fingerprint.learning: Compile-fail contract proofs for repo-owned NamedBody should use an unfiltered raw compile target instead of addFilteredTest, because --test-filter can prune the failing instantiation and make the harness fail for the wrong reason.
evidence: build.zig lexical-with boundary proof switched from addFilteredTest to raw addExecutable with expect_errors.contains
test/lexical_with_named_body_boundary_fail.zig forces compile-time NamedBody instantiation outside test-filtered blocks
fingerprint.application: When adding fail-closed proof lanes in shift, prefer raw compile targets for expected compile errors and reserve filtered tests for positive runtime behavior.

fingerprint.learning: When a shift compile-fail probe needs builtin.is_test, moving it from addExecutable to addFilteredTest also requires top-level comptime instantiation; a main()-only witness does not run in test builds, so expect_errors misses the boundary for the wrong reason.
evidence: zig build lexical-with --summary none initially failed because build.zig switched the NamedBody boundary probe to addFilteredTest but test/lexical_with_named_body_boundary_fail.zig still only instantiated FailureProbe from main(), so the expected compile error never appeared.
After test/lexical_with_named_body_boundary_fail.zig switched to a top-level comptime block, zig build lexical-with --summary none passed again under the test-mode probe.
fingerprint.application: When a build.zig compile-fail fixture must exercise builtin.is_test, keep the failure witness in unconditional comptime scope or stay on a raw executable target; do not rely on main() for test artifacts.

fingerprint.learning: $fin closeout in shift should verify PR state, branch divergence, and head SHA first; when divergence is 0 0 and GitHub reports no checks, say nothing new to push, run the local proof bundle, then merge with --match-head-commit and prove main parity.
evidence: gh pr view 20 -> mergeStateStatus=CLEAN, headRefOid=c1a7536db1fd97e8549d731b4ebd039aa57df573
git rev-list --left-right --count origin/feature/repo-owned-namedbody-fail-closed...HEAD -> 0 0; gh pr checks 20 -> no checks reported

zig fmt --check, zig build lint -- --max-warnings 0, zig build, zig build test all passed before gh pr merge --squash --delete-branch --match-head-commit c1a7536db1fd97e8549d731b4ebd039aa57df573; main...origin/main -> 0 0 after merge
fingerprint.application: Use this exact closeout truth surface for future shift $fin turns: PR metadata, divergence, remote SHA parity, local proof bundle when hosted checks are absent, guarded merge, then post-merge main parity.

fingerprint.learning: When renaming an internal module that still carries a misleading surface label, prefer renaming the file path, build module handle, import key, and compile-time test labels in one wave because partial renames leave stale identifiers behind and break proof cleanliness.
evidence: rg -n "public_" src test tools build.zig left only the exported public_runtime_errors witness field after the rename.
zig build lint -- --max-warnings 0
zig build --summary none
zig build test --summary none
fingerprint.application: For future internal naming cleanups in shift, treat module filename plus build import key plus internal fixture strings as one seam and verify the result with lint, build, and test.

fingerprint.learning: When a shift refactor only renames src/public_ir.zig and src/public_lowering.zig to src/ir_api.zig and src/lowering_api.zig, prefer treating it as symbol-compatible but path-incompatible, because the public shift.ir and shift.lowering re-exports still work while direct imports of the old file roots break.
evidence: build.zig roots the new filenames into the module graph
src/shift_shared.zig still re-exports shift.ir and shift.lowering
fingerprint.application: Use this split to judge whether a rename needs shim files or only internal call-site updates.

fingerprint.learning: $ship on an already-open shift PR should refresh the PR body to the current head and proof set; stale proof blocks can still reference a pre-fix commit even when branch parity is 0 0.
evidence: git rev-parse HEAD returned 267005078f183100570c0c4bf7b1f96b9e3204e4 while gh pr view 21 --json body still reported head 34dd971d68795f886b6fa607644216f91e4a7940 before refresh.
Rerunning zig build test --summary none, zig build public-api-compat --summary none, and a downstream local-path dependency probe succeeded before gh pr edit 21 --body-file /tmp/shift_pr_body.md.
fingerprint.application: For shift $ship turns on an existing PR, treat the body as proof state: rerun scoped validation on HEAD, replace stale proof lines with branch-head evidence, then confirm with gh pr view.

fingerprint.learning: When .learnings.jsonl is dirty during $fin, prefer verifying merged state first and using stash-based manual cleanup, because gh pr merge --delete-branch may merge remotely but abort local checkout cleanup.
evidence: gh pr merge 21 --squash --delete-branch failed local checkout on .learnings.jsonl, while gh pr view 21 showed MERGED with merge commit e3b279269431e3fd4f4887e382929fdbb7907dd5.
A stash/switch/pull/branch-delete/stash-pop/manual-remote-delete sequence completed cleanup safely.
fingerprint.application: Use this pattern to avoid losing local learnings and to avoid leaving merged feature branches undeleted after $fin.

fingerprint.learning: When a turn closes on durable-plan bookkeeping alone, keep it separate from substantive code review and treat it as orchestration-state churn, not implementation progress.
evidence: git status showed only .step/st-plan.jsonl dirty; git diff --stat showed a one-file plan-only delta.
fingerprint.application: At delivery boundaries, report the state mutation plainly and avoid inferring code-path impact unless a code file changed.

fingerprint.learning: When proving a new downstream shift consumer actually works, add a deterministic fixture-backed CLI path before live provider integration, because it exposes real file/tool/runtime issues while keeping failures local and reproducible.
evidence: zig build && zig build test && zig build invoke-fixture passed in ../actuate.
actuate demo-fixture wrote fixtures/demo_workspace/output.txt with 'actuate updated the fixture'.
fingerprint.application: Use a fixture-backed demo path as the first operational proof slice for new shift-based agents before adding live model adapters or target-repo automation.

fingerprint.learning: When publishing the first version of a new Zig agent repo, run build, test, and explicit runnable demo commands before the initial commit, because the first push should establish executable proof as well as code presence.
evidence: ../actuate passed zig build, zig build test, zig build invoke-skeleton, and zig build invoke-fixture before the initial commit.
Initial commit 8bbab2dc825a1faa2338b76f547e2d14866fcd73 pushed cleanly to origin/main with 0 0 divergence and matching ls-remote SHA.
fingerprint.application: Use runnable proof commands plus remote SHA parity as the initial publish bar for future first-version repos.

fingerprint.learning: When wiring a new Zig 0.16 OpenAI integration, route the first live call through a dedicated command and keep the deterministic path intact, because std.process and std.fs environment/file APIs have shifted enough that isolating the live path makes the integration seam obvious and debuggable.
evidence: In ../actuate, the first live path succeeded only after adapting config reads to std.Io.Dir.cwd() and giving std.http.Client an explicit io implementation.
zig build, zig build test, and zig build invoke-openai all passed after those Zig 0.16 compatibility fixes.
fingerprint.application: For future Zig 0.16 provider integrations, add a dedicated live command first, prove it separately, and treat env/file/HTTP API migration as the main early risk surface.

fingerprint.learning: When a new sibling Zig repo reports MissingApiKey during live API bringup, check the adjacent substrate repo's .env before debugging the HTTP path, because workspace secrets may still live in the older repo.
evidence: actuate invoke-openai failed with MissingApiKey until the loader fell back from ../actuate/.env to ../shift/.env, where OPENAI_API_KEY actually existed.
After adding the sibling fallback, zig build, zig build test, and zig build invoke-openai succeeded.
fingerprint.application: Use explicit sibling .env fallback during workspace bringup for new API-backed repos.

fingerprint.learning: When adding the first real external tool to a new Zig agent, prefer a dedicated command and a no-key API first, because it proves tool routing and live IO without entangling the result with extra auth surfaces.
evidence: ../actuate now answers a live weather question through Open-Meteo plus the OpenAI-backed finalizer.
zig build, zig build test, and zig build invoke-weather succeeded, and the CLI returned a concrete San Francisco weather answer.
fingerprint.application: Use a dedicated low-friction external tool as the first live tool slice before broadening into a general tool router.

fingerprint.learning: When turning a shift architecture plan into durable execution, prefer a new numbered campaign of fixed-point-driver-sized slices with executor-specific notes, keep historical waves in_plan=false, and mirror only the active campaign into update_plan.
evidence: .step/st-plan.jsonl now carries st-1500..st-1505 as the only in_plan items while older completed campaigns remain off-plan.
st show --file .step/st-plan.jsonl --format markdown reports only st-1500 ready and later items waiting on typed deps.
fingerprint.application: Use a fresh campaign range plus notes for executor/proof constraints instead of reopening older completed slices or collapsing the work into one monolithic durable item.

fingerprint.learning: When hard-cutting shift ArtifactV1 host contracts to a new public runtime surface, prefer a committed artifact-bytes fixture plus a runtime-only smoke and direct bridge tests, because compile-coupled smoke can let encoder and decoder drift together without exposing public-contract regressions.
evidence: test/fixtures/shift_agent_vm_smoke.artifact is now generated by zig build generate-shift-agent-vm-fixture and replayed by test/shift_agent_vm_public_smoke_test.zig through shift_agent_vm.runtime.runArtifact.
src/shift_agent_vm.zig now has direct tests for after_call classification and failed-response request-id preservation alongside the green local proof bundle.
fingerprint.application: When a future shift public runtime cutover changes the host bridge or artifact version contract, add one committed artifact-bytes witness and one direct bridge witness before calling the new surface stable.

fingerprint.learning: When a shift bridge helper duplicates one owned field and then clones another allocator-owned payload, prefer a shared builder with errdefer cleanup on each temporary, because HostEffectResultV1 deinit cannot recover allocations leaked before the union value is fully constructed.
evidence: src/shift_agent_vm.zig responseToInternal now routes .resumed/.return_now/.aborted through cloneSuccessResult with errdefer on tool_id and cloned_value.
zig build test -- --test-filter "response bridge frees duplicated tool ids on allocation failure" passed after adding std.testing.checkAllAllocationFailures coverage.
fingerprint.application: For future shift bridge/result builders, collapse repeated success-arm construction into one helper and add allocation-failure witnesses whenever partially-built temporaries could otherwise leak.

fingerprint.learning: When a public compatibility shim mirrors internal request/response ownership and deinit logic, prefer collapsing translation helpers rather than widening the underlying artifact contract, because the shim's extra state is incidental while the codec's validation and binary fidelity are essential.
evidence: src/shift_agent_vm.zig:22-197 duplicates ownership flags and bridge functions across host/runtime wrappers
src/agent_vm_artifact.zig:117-188,383-610 keeps the essential encode/decode/validate protocol logic in one codec surface
test/shift_agent_vm_public_smoke_test.zig:20-73 proves the seam is exercised through a compatibility test rather than direct protocol expansion
fingerprint.application: On the next seam audit, keep the codec module fixed and simplify the adapter translation layer first.

fingerprint.learning: When restoring ArtifactV1 v2 compatibility after a v3 capability-manifest cutover, version-gate both capability-op row decoding and fingerprint hashing, and carry the decoded artifact version through ArtifactV1.validate so legacy manifests do not silently rehash under the wrong domain.
evidence: src/agent_vm_artifact.zig now decodes v2 capability op rows from legacy tool.call/tool.after string refs while keeping v3 host_op_kind bytes for current artifacts.
src/agent_vm_artifact.zig now recomputes capability fingerprints with per-version domains and stores artifact_version on ArtifactV1 so validate() uses the same domain as decode().

zig build test --summary none -- --test-filter="ArtifactV1 decode preserves v2 capability manifests and fingerprint domain" passed, alongside zig build lint -- --max-warnings 0, zig build --summary none, and zig build test --summary none.
fingerprint.application: On future ArtifactV1 wire-version bumps, treat decode layout, fingerprint domain, and post-decode validation as one compatibility unit and add at least one non-empty legacy-manifest witness.

fingerprint.learning: When a public adapter success response can carry its own request_id, prefer rejecting mismatches at the bridge before cloning into the internal envelope, because re-stamping the internal request_id hides contract drift and lets happy-path-only tests miss the bug.
evidence: src/shift_agent_vm.zig:275-276 now rejects mismatched success request_ids; src/artifact_vm_runtime.zig:578-587 still enforces internal echo checks; test/shift_agent_vm_public_smoke_test.zig:20-74 is happy-path only.
fingerprint.application: Add a negative bridge test for mismatched success request_id and keep runtime echo checks separate from bridge validation.

fingerprint.learning: When stabilizing shift_agent_vm as a public ArtifactV1 runtime surface, prefer converting completed values and captured transcripts into host.DataValue plus host.Request/Response shapes instead of aliasing artifact_vm_runtime result structs, because otherwise downstream callers stay coupled to private runtime and wire types.
evidence: src/shift_agent_vm.zig now converts ExecutionResult/HostFailureResult/RunArtifactResult and the public smoke test asserts completed.logs[0].request and completed.logs[0].response on the retained runtime surface.
zig build lint -- --max-warnings 0, zig build --summary none, zig build test --summary none, and zig build public-api-compat --summary none all passed after the bridge.
fingerprint.application: For future public-surface compatibility work, treat request/response bridging and success/log/result projection as separate closure obligations and pin both with public-surface tests.

fingerprint.learning: When a public shift_agent_vm bridge deep-clones outputs or logged responses, prefer explicit allocation-failure tests for each partial-clone boundary, because paired label/tool-id and payload allocations can otherwise leak or double-free under OOM-only paths.
evidence: This turn fixed partial-allocation cleanup in src/shift_agent_vm.zig for executionOutputsFromInternal, hostLogsFromInternal, and responseFromInternal, then reran the targeted allocation-failure public-api lane until it passed.

Validation after the fixes passed: zig build public-api-compat --summary none -- --test-filter "allocation failure", zig build public-api-compat --summary none -- --test-filter "shift_agent_vm public smoke", zig build lint -- --max-warnings 0, zig build --summary none, zig build test --summary none, zig build public-api-compat --summary none.
fingerprint.application: On future public compatibility shims in shift, treat allocator-failure cleanup as a first-class review surface and add checkAllAllocationFailures coverage at every owned clone boundary before declaring the bridge closed.

fingerprint.learning: When a Zig surface must stay source-compatible without becoming a package-level registration, use build.zig createModule plus explicit test imports; otherwise addModule can make the surface look publicly published by name even though the build graph is the only owner.
evidence: build.zig:4021-4025 creates shift_agent_vm_mod with createModule
build.zig:4534-4555 wires shift_agent_vm into path-compatibility and public smoke tests
src/shift_agent_vm_path_compatibility_test.zig:3-9 checks source-path compatibility
test/shift_agent_vm_public_smoke_test.zig:1-125 imports shift_agent_vm and exercises runtime.runArtifact
fingerprint.application: Use when deciding whether a build graph surface should be treated as published or only as an internal module object.

fingerprint.learning: When smoke tests touch the committed fixture path, regenerate from through , because the test only proves the runtime wrapper against the stored bytes and stale artifacts can mask fixture drift.
evidence: build.zig wires and as separate targets
generate_shift_agent_vm_fixture.zig compiles into
shift_agent_vm_public_smoke_test.zig reads the committed artifact and exercises
fingerprint.application: Refresh the artifact whenever the source fixture or lowering path changes, then compare the regenerated bytes before trusting the smoke result

fingerprint.learning: When a retained Zig source-path surface must coexist with a build-wired module graph, prefer proving the contract with an ordinary consumer compile and routing the in-tree build graph through private build-wired copies, because forcing one import regime to satisfy both surfaces reintroduces file-to-module ownership collisions.
evidence: zig build shift-agent-vm-compat --summary none passed after switching the compat step to shift_agent_vm_source_path_consumer.zig and rooting the build graph at src/private_modules/*_build.zig copies.
fingerprint.application: For future retained source-path compatibility work, split the public source-path files from the private build-wired module roots early and keep the compatibility lane scoped to the ordinary consumer compile.

fingerprint.learning: When adding a new public Zig surface in shift, use b.addModule(...) for the published package entry and wire at least one executable smoke lane into build.zig, because b.createModule(...) plus a compile-only compatibility step leaves package consumers and normal CI unable to catch regressions.
evidence: build.zig creates shift_agent_vm with b.createModule at lines 4021-4024 instead of exposing it as a named package module.
The new shift-agent-vm-compat step at build.zig:4569-4573 only compiles shift_agent_vm_source_path_consumer.zig and does not run the new smoke test files.
fingerprint.application: For future public-surface additions, prove both package importability and runtime behavior from standard build/test entrypoints before treating the API as shipped.

fingerprint.learning: When shift publishes a new Zig package module, prefer pairing b.addModule(...) with a repo-owned downstream dependency.module(...) witness, because same-graph addImport smokes can stay green even if the package export regresses.
evidence: This turn fixed shift_agent_vm publication by switching build.zig to b.addModule("shift_agent_vm", ...) and wiring the retained smoke/path suite into public-api-compat and zig build test.

A new build.zig unit test now creates a temp consumer package under .zig-cache/tmp and proves dep.module("shift_agent_vm") builds successfully via zig test build.zig --test-filter "shift_agent_vm package module is exported to downstream dependency consumers".
fingerprint.application: For future public-module additions in shift, keep one runtime/path witness in normal build steps and one downstream dependency.module witness in repo-owned proof so export regressions fail closed.

fingerprint.learning: When adding a compatibility witness for a retained surface, prefer wiring it into the build step or deleting it, because an unwired duplicate wrapper only adds incidental surface and forces readers to reconcile two names for the same contract.
evidence: review_shift_agent_vm_consumer.zig is untracked and duplicates the existing source-path consumer pattern, while build.zig has no reference to it and the public compatibility suite already covers the retained shift_agent_vm path and smoke checks.
fingerprint.application: For future compat lanes, keep exactly one build-owned witness per distinct contract unless the extra file is explicitly part of the validation graph.

fingerprint.learning: When a retained module surface needs both source-path importability and downstream package consumption proof, prefer a single compatibility step that bundles the source-path witness, smoke test, and dependency-consumer build, because splitting them makes the strongest verification easy to miss in future review or closeout passes.
evidence: build.zig wired shift-agent-vm-compat to shift_agent_vm_source_path_consumer.zig, src/shift_agent_vm_path_compatibility_test.zig, and test/shift_agent_vm_public_smoke_test.zig.
zig test build.zig --test-filter "shift_agent_vm" passed, and zig build shift-agent-vm-compat --summary none passed.
fingerprint.application: Reuse this pattern for other retained package modules that need both import-shape and downstream-consumer proof.

fingerprint.learning: When a committed artifact-bytes witness for shift_agent_vm starts depending on nested build context instead of the product contract, remove it from the default test suite and keep the ordinary consumer compile, runtime smoke, and downstream package export proofs.
evidence: zig build shift-agent-vm-compat --summary none passed after removing test/shift_agent_vm_fixture_bytes_test.zig from build.zig wiring

fingerprint.application: Prefer generator-native diff steps or opt-in refresh lanes for committed bytes; keep default proof waves on stable consumer/runtime contracts.

fingerprint.learning: When a retained shift compat suite replays checked-in artifact bytes, make the replay run depend on fixture regeneration and commit any refreshed fixture bytes in the same review wave; otherwise compile-to-runtime drift can pass unnoticed behind stale artifacts.
evidence: build.zig now makes run_shift_agent_vm_smoke depend on run_shift_agent_vm_fixture before shift-agent-vm-compat/public-api-compat.
Re-running the compat lane regenerated test/fixtures/shift_agent_vm_smoke.artifact to new bytes while all closure gates stayed green.
fingerprint.application: For artifact-backed compat witnesses in shift, wire generation into the consuming run step, rerun the owning compat suite, and stage both the build-graph edge and any resulting committed fixture refresh.

fingerprint.learning: When adding a new shift benchmark that compares lexical with program execution, wire the benchmark step to shift_shared rather than bench_support; bench_support preserves older benchmark helpers but does not expose with/Decl/run for the two-lane contract.
evidence: zig build zprof-lane-compare --summary none succeeded only after importing shift_shared for the new zprof lane bench, producing matching lexical/program checksums and allocation counts.
fingerprint.application: For future lane-comparison or root-contract benchmarks, import shift_shared (or the exact public root under test) instead of reusing bench_support by default.

fingerprint.learning: When turning a shift architecture collapse plan into durable execution for fixed-point-driver, prefer a fresh numbered st campaign with backlog-only steps, explicit fixed-point-driver executor notes, and an st-1600 root item that records any later Wave 2 work as out of scope so the execution driver does not silently blend phases.
evidence: In this turn, the durable ledger added st-1600 through st-1606 as backlog-only items in .step/st-plan.jsonl, with st-1600 marked ready and later items dependency-gated for fixed-point-driver execution.
fingerprint.application: For future architecture-to-execution handoffs in shift, create a new numbered st campaign instead of mutating older historical slices, keep the mirrored plan empty until execution starts, and encode deferred follow-on waves explicitly in the root campaign item.

fingerprint.learning: When shift.with remains type-based, collapsing NamedBody and lexical_runtime_internal requires every surviving example and witness callsite that passed a bare body function to wrap it in an inline body type, and caller_source preview checks must treat literal null as the canonical absent provenance shape after the explicit-caller lane is removed.
evidence: zig build --summary none failed on examples/state_basic.zig and other examples until bare function bodies were wrapped
zig build test --summary none failed on test/lexical_with_preview_test.zig until caller_source .null was treated as absent
fingerprint.application: For future one-lane lexical cleanup, scan examples/tests for direct function bodies passed to shift.with and normalize preview assertions over caller_source to handle null as well as optional null.

fingerprint.learning: When shift narrows the @import("shift") root API, rename proof lanes away from broad compatibility wording and document the root export set explicitly so retained sibling-module checks are not mistaken for root-surface compatibility.
evidence: build.zig renamed public-api-compat to published-module-contract and updated the suite id/description.
README.md now lists only shift.effect, shift.Runtime, shift.RuntimeError, and shift.with as the root export set.
Proof: zig build lint -- --max-warnings 0; zig build published-module-contract --summary none; zig build test --summary none
fingerprint.application: For future public-surface reductions, update the build step name/description and README contract wording in the same patch.

fingerprint.learning: When a user asks whether all shift builds, lints, fmts, and tests pass, prefer rerunning the full standard closure live with zig fmt --check ., zig build lint -- --max-warnings 0, zig build --summary none, and zig build test --summary none because narrower proof lanes can overstate closure.
evidence: Verification in this turn ran zig fmt --check ., zig build lint -- --max-warnings 0, zig build --summary none, and zig build test --summary none; all exited 0.
fingerprint.application: For future closure-status questions in shift, answer from the explicit full gate set rather than from earlier targeted suite results.

fingerprint.learning: When gh pr merge reports a dirty-worktree checkout failure during shift fin closeout, prefer checking whether the PR already merged and then finish cleanup with a targeted stash because the remote squash merge may already be complete.
evidence: gh pr merge 23 failed local checkout because .learnings.jsonl and test/fixtures/shift_agent_vm_smoke.artifact were dirty
gh pr view 23 immediately after the failure reported state=MERGED and mergeCommit=77509d96723fd91c0bee86796c132e7a411a06ae
fingerprint.application: For shift fin closeout, treat that failure as cleanup-only: stash the dirty paths, fetch and fast-forward main, delete the merged local branch, and reapply the stash.

fingerprint.learning: When reviving whole-body ProgramPlan execution for plain shift.with(...) after removing explicit caller-source APIs, restore the anonymous-body source-synthesis seam first and keep branch-stale baseline fixes separate, because otherwise provenance design work gets conflated with unrelated generic body inference or stale bench/toolchain failures.
evidence: src/with_api.zig callBody now uses BodyReturnType(Body, @TypeOf(eff)), which restored zig build run-state-basic, zig build run-optional-basic, and zig build run-exception-basic on the current branch.

src/internal/anonymous_body_synthesis.zig now restores offsetForLineColumn/bodyExpressionBounds/syntheticSourceWithEntry as an isolated internal seam, while commit af2b6ca shows the previous compiled path depended on that style of caller-provenance-driven synthesis feeding public_lowering.maybeLower(...).
fingerprint.application: For future shift executor-convergence turns, fix stale baseline breakage first, then rebuild provenance and synthetic-source helpers as standalone internals before attempting the full with_api ProgramPlan cutover.

fingerprint.learning: When introducing a shared AdmittedBodyV1 parser in shift, keep it runtime-capable for source_graph_engine and preserve helper(eff) or helper(..., eff) zero-value-arg semantics or the public compiled with(...) seam regresses immediately.
evidence: src/internal/admitted_body_v1.zig now serves both source_graph_engine and helper_body_lowering, so comptime-only parser parameters broke full test compilation until removed.
Normalizing helper(eff) as a normal value arg caused build failures on the public compiled seam until helper trailing-eff stripping matched the old helper-call contract.
fingerprint.application: When centralizing scanner/lowerer admission logic, port the old helper-arg contract first and make the shared parser callable from both runtime and comptime analysis paths before widening witness forms.

fingerprint.learning: When a shift lexical witness anonymous body fails the retained compiled subset because it uses inline loop, nested with, or other rich control flow, prefer extracting that logic into top-level helper functions and keeping the anonymous body as a helper call before widening AdmittedBodyV1.
evidence: Generator stopped hitting the immediate retained-subset compile gate after lexical_witness_support moved the inline while-plus-switch body into lexicalWitnessEmitFirst/Second/Third helper functions.
Static redelim and multi-prompt were then normalized to helper-backed bodies so the anonymous lexical body surface matched the canonical witness-source pattern.

fingerprint.application: For future ProgramPlan codec fixes, distinguish payload-valued plain completion, after-capable completion, terminal-only escape, and unit completion before changing function.result_codec.

fingerprint.learning: When ProgramPlan helper result_codec inference sees both plain value completion and after-transformed completion for the same non-diagonal helper, prefer rejecting the mixed completion shape instead of stamping one function-wide completion codec because callers and runtimes have single-codec destination locals.
evidence: src/internal/program_plan.zig and src/private_modules/internal_program_plan_build.zig now compute plain/after completion reachability and return InvalidFunctionResultCodec or InvalidProgramBodyShape for mixed helper completions.
Focused regression passed: zig build test -Dtest-suites=program-plan-review --summary none -- --test-filter 'mixed plain and after completion codecs'.
Full gates passed before commit: zig build lint -- --max-warnings 0, zig build --summary none, zig build test --summary none, zig build check-ability-agent-vm-fixture --summary none.
fingerprint.application: For future ProgramPlan result_codec review fixes, model completion codec agreement path-sensitively first and fail closed on mixed non-diagonal helper completions unless caller/runtime storage becomes path-sensitive.

fingerprint.learning: When ProgramPlan runtimes support helpers with separate completion and terminal codecs, keep public entry dispatch strict and route helper-only unit completions through an internal completion mode because a global unit-result exception lets decoded entry plans return .none for non-unit answers.
evidence: Fixed review comments by adding UnitCompletionMode in src/internal/program_plan_interpreter.zig and src/artifact_vm_runtime.zig; full zig build test passed after custom-effect workflow initially failed on void helper terminal paths.
fingerprint.application: For future result_codec repairs, distinguish public/entry strict result decoding from recursive helper completion semantics before changing unwindAfterStack.

fingerprint.learning: When an Ability audit accepts both report-tool CLI and ArtifactV1 conformance concerns, split the stable build/install step from the runtime classifier step because host-call fixtures are expected to classify unsupported with a nonzero run result.
evidence: zig build agent-vm-artifact-report --summary none passed and ./zig-out/bin/agent-vm-artifact-report --version printed 0.0.2
zig build run-agent-vm-artifact-report -Dagent-vm-artifact=test/fixtures/ability_agent_vm_smoke.artifact --summary none emitted verdict=unsupported and exited 1
fingerprint.application: For future agent-vm-artifact-report changes, keep zig build agent-vm-artifact-report as an installable binary proof and use run-agent-vm-artifact-report only for classification fixtures where unsupported may be the correct verdict.

fingerprint.learning: When README source-witness examples are meant to be copy-runnable, prefer probing them in a downstream package before shipping because ability.with validates source_file/source_location/source_identity against Zig's actual module-root path semantics.
evidence: Temp consumer /tmp/ability-readme-consumer.3c9pbi: source_file derived from @src().file or set to src/main.zig failed with 'source_identity/source_file/source_location did not match'; source_file = main.zig then zig build run --summary none printed 9.

Current-tree gates passed: zig fmt --check build.zig src examples test bench source_graph_embed.zig tools; zig build lint -- --max-warnings 0; zig build check-ability-agent-vm-fixture --summary none; zig build --summary none; zig build test --summary none.
fingerprint.application: For future downstream docs or package-consumer contract edits, create a throwaway consumer build and run the documented snippet before treating README changes as resolved.

fingerprint.learning: When source-lowered ProgramPlans need after-hook parity, prefer inferring has_after from explicit continuation-shaped direct op uses instead of blanket-marking non-abort ops, because blanket metadata can violate unrelated comptime/runtime contracts.
evidence: custom-effect workflow direct ProgramPlan approval branch initially missed afterRequest continuation; blanket non-abort has_after made the focused lane pass but failed the broader comptime-contract regression bundle.

Final proof: zig build test -Dtest-suites=custom-effect-workflow,comptime-contract,source-ownership-probe,lexical-with,program-plan-review --summary all passed 68/68; zig build test --summary all passed 337/337.
fingerprint.application: Route future source-lowering after-hook fixes through source graph continuation witnesses and fixed-point regression bundles before widening row metadata.

fingerprint.learning: When source-lowered after-hook parity reviews mention helper/caller leakage, keep has_after function-local: infer it from direct continuation uses only, let used ops propagate separately, and prove with a mixed caller/helper regression.
evidence: src/lowering_api.zig removes helper-edge propagation from inferAfterOps and adds mixed caller/helper after metadata regression
zig build test --summary none -- --test-filter 'caller-owned lowering keeps helper after metadata off unrelated caller direct ops' passed
zig build lint -- --max-warnings 0, zig build, and zig build test --summary none passed
fingerprint.application: For future source-lowering after-hook fixes, separate operation reachability from after-frame ownership and add a fixture where caller and helper share the same op but only one has an explicit continuation.

fingerprint.learning: When fixing truncated source-graph statement inference, prefer shape-specific fail-closed guards over rejecting every overflowing statement because long valid nested source patterns may also exceed the token window.
evidence: zig build test initially failed with nested lexical named carrier ParseError after broad StatementWindow overflow rejection; narrowed source_graph_engine.zig guard then passed zig build test, zig build lint -- --max-warnings 0, and zig build.
fingerprint.application: On source-graph overflow reviews, first classify the overflowing statement shape and only fail closed for the shape that can silently corrupt inference.

fingerprint.learning: When source-lowered after-hook inference sees one function use the same op both directly and with an explicit continuation, reject that shape before ProgramPlan generation unless the row authored has_after, because current plan metadata is function/op scoped and cannot preserve callsite-specific after semantics.
evidence: src/lowering_api.zig adds mixedAfterConflict/rejectUnrepresentableMixedAfterOps and a same-function mixed direct/continuation regression.

Proof: zig build test -Dtest-suites=custom-effect-workflow --summary all passed 7/7; zig build lint -- --max-warnings 0 passed; zig build --summary all passed; zig build test --summary all passed 337/337.
fingerprint.application: For future source-lowering after-hook reviews, choose fail-closed mixed-use rejection unless the implementation also splits op metadata per callsite/instruction.

fingerprint.learning: When source-lowering review comments target graph-level direct-op guards, prefer scoping blocking validation to reachableFunctions before rejecting because buildFunctionsForGraph emits only reachable bodies.
evidence: Fixed unreachable mixed-after rejection in src/lowering_api.zig; focused gate fail->pass: zig build test -- --test-filter 'caller-owned lowering ignores unreachable mixed direct and continuation after metadata'.
fingerprint.application: Add a dead-function fixture plus a still-reachable conflict fixture whenever a source graph guard scans graph.direct_op_uses.

fingerprint.learning: When a public maybe-lowering probe shares a helper with a hard lowering builder, add the unsupported-shape preflight at the maybe boundary before the hard builder can emit @compileError, because maybeLower callers depend on null for retained-subset misses.
evidence: Review comment on src/lowering_api.zig mixed direct plus explicit-continuation after metadata: hard buildFunctionsForGraph guard made maybeLower compile-error instead of null.

Validation passed: zig build test -Dtest-suites=root --summary none -- --test-filter 'maybe lowering returns null for same-function mixed direct and continuation after metadata'; zig build lint -- --max-warnings 0; zig build --summary none; zig build test --summary none; zig test build.zig.
fingerprint.application: For future maybeLower support probes, route new unrepresentable retained-subset guards through a nullable preflight for maybe APIs while leaving lower/lowerAt fail-closed.

fingerprint.learning: When adding direct runtime regression tests for ArtifactV1 host dispatch, do echo request-owned ids and set allocator-owned response values to deep ownership because standalone zig test runs expose host-log clone leaks that filtered build suites can miss.
evidence: zig test src/artifact_vm_runtime.zig --test-filter 'artifact runtime resolves reordered repeated-requirement after hooks by capability id' failed first on call_id echo, then on leaked response value ownership, then passed after request-owned call_id and value_ownership=.deep fixes.
fingerprint.application: For future ArtifactV1 runtime tests, make host mock responses obey request_id/tool_id/call_id echo invariants and ownership flags, then run the direct zig test in addition to build-suite filters.

fingerprint.learning: When Ability runtime regressions live in private build modules with curated default filters, add the new regression name to the build.zig default filter list because otherwise full zig build test can miss the proof despite a source-level test.
evidence: Patched src/private_modules/artifact_vm_runtime_build.zig with test 'artifact runtime forwards raised artifact byte budget into decode' and added 'raised artifact byte budget' to build.zig vm_build_filters.
Proof: zig build test -Dtest-suites=artifact-vm-runtime-build-host-log-budget --summary none and zig build test --summary none both passed.
fingerprint.application: For future ArtifactV1/runtime fixes in ability, inspect build.zig suite filters and wire new private-module tests into default filters before claiming full-suite coverage.

fingerprint.learning: When Ability audit remediation follows a full-suite review, prefer narrowing to a CLI/report contract cluster before patching because broad API opacity or unmeasured performance findings can swamp a shippable proof wave.
evidence: Accepted only source-lower/report diagnostics and verdict-detail work; deferred Runtime opacity and performance findings.
Proof passed: focused source-lowering-tool,agent-vm-artifact-report; check-ability-agent-vm-fixture; fmt; lint; zig build; zig build test.
fingerprint.application: For future Ability audit waves, route user-facing CLI/report contract findings first, keep broad API/perf findings deferred unless measured or explicitly scoped, and prove with focused plus full Zig gates.

fingerprint.learning: When a stable CLI diagnostic whitelist mirrors an error set, compare the whitelist against the source error declaration and add a direct same-module regression for the exact admitted string because typo-only fixes otherwise preserve a future drift footgun.
evidence: tools/agent_vm_artifact_report.zig changed MissingRequiredSection to InvalidRequiredSection after src/private_modules/agent_vm_artifact_build.zig DecodeError showed InvalidRequiredSection.
Proof gates passed: zig fmt --check ., zig build lint -- --max-warnings 0, zig build test, zig build.
fingerprint.application: For future report/diagnostic whitelist review fixes, inspect the authoritative error set first, remove stale names, and pin the admitted detail with a focused same-module test.

fingerprint.learning: When adding a new native runtime failure mode in ability, propagate the error through the public lowered-machine RuntimeError and error-witness surfaces before shipping because public with_api paths cast lowered execution errors into that reset error set.
evidence: One-change challenge after full gates found ExecutionBudgetExceeded added to ProgramPlan execution but absent from src/lowered_machine.zig RuntimeError and src/error_witness.zig, risking @errorCast failure in with_api.zig.
Closure after propagation: zig build lint -- --max-warnings 0, zig build, and zig build test all passed.
fingerprint.application: On future runtime-envelope changes, review program_plan_interpreter, lowering_api, lowered_machine, private_modules/lowered_machine_build, error_witness, and with_api error-cast callers as one contract cluster.

fingerprint.learning: When adding review regressions for budget propagation through nested execution, prefer making the old reset path otherwise succeed so the test fails only on the missing shared budget.
evidence: src/internal/program_plan_interpreter.zig regression was tightened to give call_nested_with a valid i32 destination after verifier found the first version had a post-nested ProgramContractViolation confound; final gates passed with zig build lint -- --max-warnings 0, zig build, and zig build test.
fingerprint.application: For future budget/resource-envelope review fixes, construct focused tests where all non-budget contracts are valid before asserting the expected budget error.

fingerprint.learning: When ProgramPlan native execution queues after hooks, prefer reserving the after-frame slot before invoking an after-capable handler because append-time charging lets exhausted budgets leak handler side effects.
evidence: src/internal/program_plan_interpreter.zig now reserves via opRequiresAfterFrame before callOp; focused tests for max_after_frames=0 and one pending frame passed; full zig fmt/lint/build/test bundle passed.
fingerprint.application: For future after-hook or continuation-budget fixes, add side-effect counter regressions that prove exhausted budgets fail before handler dispatch, not only before unwind.

fingerprint.learning: When Ability audit remediation includes low-severity CLI exit-code findings, prefer verifying the exact user-visible wrapper before patching because Zig build can mask child process exit codes.
evidence: During audit fixed-point remediation, changing test/generate_ability_agent_vm_fixture.zig child usage exit to 2 did not change 'zig build generate-ability-agent-vm-fixture -- --bad', which still exited 1 through the build wrapper.
The no-op child-exit patch was reverted; accepted remediation stayed on source-lower duplicate flags, agent-vm report output-mode conflicts, and lowering_api recovery copy.
fingerprint.application: Before accepting a CLI exit-code audit item, run the cited command shape first and patch the visible boundary, not just the child tool.

fingerprint.learning: When fixed-point specialist output violates the packet contract, prefer rejecting it as closure evidence while independently verifying any concrete blocker before proceeding.

fingerprint.application: Record the packet as transport-invalid, rerun the named proof lane locally, then patch only the verified blocker and rerun closure gates.

fingerprint.learning: When Ability audit remediation adds fail-closed build-option misuse paths after parsing allocator-owned selections, prefer deiniting the parsed selection before returning invalid_user_input because the fixed-point one-change challenge can catch small build-allocator leaks before ship.

proof gates passed afterward: zig build --summary none; zig build test --summary none; zig build lint -- --max-warnings 0
fingerprint.application: In future Ability build.zig UX fixes, check parsed selection ownership on every early invalid_user_input return.

fingerprint.learning: When build.zig test-specific option validation runs during review remediation, prefer threading skip-execution discovery state into the option resolver because help/list/fetch workflows must tolerate carried test flags while real non-test execution remains fail-closed.
evidence: Review repro passed: zig build --list-steps -Dtest-suites=source-ownership-probe and zig build --list-steps -Dtest-suites=does-not-exist both exited 0 after threading skip_execution into resolveTestSuiteSelection.
Fail-closed side preserved: zig build source-lower -Dtest-suites=root exited 1 with the documented test-suites-only error.
fingerprint.application: On future build.zig option guards, separate discovery/help/fetch skip-execution from runnable step ownership before parsing or rejecting option values.

fingerprint.learning: When proving Ability ArtifactV1 capability-manifest invariants, add a build-wired public compile-suite test instead of relying on direct `zig test src/agent_vm_artifact.zig`, because the direct source test path can fail on comptime-only ProgramPlan imports before exercising the invariant.

`zig build test -Dtest-suites=agent-vm-artifact-report,ability-agent-vm-smoke,source-lowering-tool,comptime-contract --summary none` passed after adding the public compile-suite regression
fingerprint.application: For future ArtifactV1 contract fixes, put the executable regression in an existing build.zig test suite such as comptime-contract or a dedicated build-wired suite, and treat direct source tests as optional diagnostics only.

fingerprint.learning: When Ability audit remediation recall identifies a prior successful CLI/report contract route, use that memory to narrow adjudication only after current-source claim checks, because broad API/build/perf findings can be real but not ship-worthy without fresh compatibility or profiling evidence.

fingerprint.learning: When wrapping expected-failing CLI smoke checks in zsh, avoid assigning to the special read-only status parameter; capture exit codes in a neutral name like rc so the wrapper does not mask the CLI result.
evidence: During agent-vm-artifact-report smoke verification, zsh reported 'read-only variable: status' after the tool had emitted the expected JSON error; rerunning with rc captured exit:2.
fingerprint.application: Use rc or exit_code in zsh smoke wrappers that need to preserve a failing command's output and exit status.
